property.property_specification: Whenever strong wind conditions are present, the payload lifter remains functional.

property.property_specification: Whenever the switch is flipped, the copter eventually lands at the launch location.

property.property_specification: Whenever a switch is flipped, the copter will eventually land automatically at the launch location.

property.reason: The process is described as inevitable once AUTO mode is activated, requiring 'A<>' to assert that landing and disarming eventually occur in all paths.

property.property_specification: Switching the vehicle to AUTO will eventually lead to the vehicle landing.
property.reason: The text specifies that after switching to AUTO, the vehicle will land.

property.property_specification: Autonomous vehicles are potentially dangerous.

property.property_specification: Eventually the copter will be disarmed.

property.reason: Expresses that the 'disarmed' state will inevitably be reached.

property.property_specification: Setting H_RSC_AROT_RAMP leads to throttle returning to the curve within 1 to 2 seconds.

property.property_specification: If H_RSC_AROT_RAMP is set, then eventually the throttle will return to the throttle curve.

property.reason: Expresses that setting H_RSC_AROT_RAMP implies that the throttle will reach throttle curve in one to two seconds, using clock variable x.

property.property_specification: If the altitude exceeds the maximum or minimum limit, then a fence breach will eventually be declared in all modes.
property.reason: The text states that exceeding altitude limits leads to a fence breach declaration in all modes. This is a 'leads to' relationship between the altitude violation and the breach state.

property.property_specification: If the altitude exceeds the maximum or minimum limit, a fence breach will be declared in all modes.
property.reason: Represents the 'leads to' relationship: when altitude limits are exceeded, it inevitably leads to a fence breach.

property.property_specification: For Plane, when FENCE_OPTIONS is 1 and a fence breach occurs, mode changes are not allowed until the vehicle returns within the fence boundary.
property.uppaal_formula: A[] ((vehicle_type == Plane && FENCE_OPTIONS == 1 && fence_breach) imply (mode_change_allowed == false))
property.reason: Ensures mode changes are blocked for Plane with FENCE_OPTIONS=1 during a breach until recovery.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_OPTIONS is set to "1", then after a fence breach, no mode changes are allowed until the vehicle returns within the fence boundary.
property.reason: Expresses a leads to relationship: if the fence option is 1 then eventually the vehicle will return inside the fence, assuming mode changes happen only within the location 'vehicle_within_fence'.

property.property_specification: FENCE_ALT_MAX is unavailable in Rover mode.
property.reason: Asserts that whenever the system is in Rover mode, FENCE_ALT_MAX is unset (unavailable), assuming 0 represents the default/unavailable value.

property.property_specification: Setting FENCE_ALT_MAX is unavailable in Rover.
property.reason: The text suggests that the action of setting FENCE_ALT_MAX is impossible or irrelevant when the system is in the Rover state. 'A[] !Rover' means that the system is always not in the Rover state when setting FENCE_ALT_MAX.

property.property_specification: The vehicle must always maintain a distance greater than or equal to FENCE_MARGIN from the fence boundary to prevent a breach.
property.reason: Defines an invariant safety property: the vehicle's distance from the fence boundary must never fall below FENCE_MARGIN to avoid a breach.

property.property_specification: The vehicle must always maintain a distance greater than or equal to OA_MARGIN_MAX from fences and stay-out zones.
property.reason: The requirement specifies a safety invariant (always staying at least OA_MARGIN_MAX meters away from restricted zones), which maps directly to an A[] temporal operator enforcing the condition in all states.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_AUTOENABLE is non-zero, FENCE_ENABLE is ignored.
property.reason: When FENCE_AUTOENABLE is enabled (non-zero), FENCE_ENABLE has no effect (leads-to relationship).

property.property_specification: FENCE_AUTOENABLE = 0 disables automatic fence enabling.
property.reason: A value of 0 ensures the automatic fence feature is inactive (invariant).

property.property_specification: FENCE_AUTOENABLE being greater than 0 implies fencing_enabled will eventually holds.
property.reason: Using leads to: if FENCE_AUTOENABLE is greater than zero then fencing_enabled.

property.property_specification: FENCE_AUTOENABLE = 3 enables the fence when the vehicle is armed.
property.reason: FENCE_AUTOENABLE = 3 triggers fence enabling upon arming (leads-to relationship).

property.property_specification: If FENCE_AUTOENABLE is enabled and the vehicle is armed, then fencing is enabled.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the auto-enable feature is on and the vehicle is armed, then fencing will eventually be enabled.

property.property_specification: The system must always maintain a cylindrical fence radius of at least 30m.
property.reason: The text specifies a minimum radius requirement, which translates to an invariant (A[]) that the radius variable must always be greater than or equal to 30.

property.property_specification: The fence radius is always at least 30 meters
property.reason: Expresses a safety invariant that the fence radius never violates the minimum recommended value of 30 meters

property.property_specification: The cylindrical fence radius must always be greater than or equal to 30.
property.reason: Expressing the constraint that the radius must always be greater than or equal to 30 using the invariant A[] operator.

property.property_specification: While the fence is enabled, the GPS arming check and EKF failsafe must not be disabled.
property.reason: Ensures the GPS and EKF checks remain enabled whenever the fence is enabled (safety invariant).

property.property_specification: If the fence is enabled, then the GPS arming check must not be disabled.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: if the fence is enabled, the GPS arming check must not be disabled eventually.

property.property_specification: If the fence is enabled, then the EKF failsafe must not be disabled.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: if the fence is enabled, the EKF failsafe must not be disabled eventually.

property.property_specification: If either the GPS arming check or the EKF failsafe is disabled, the fence must be disabled.
property.reason: Ensures disabling either check forces the fence to be disabled (contrapositive safety invariant).

property.property_specification: If the GPS arming check is disabled, then the fence must be disabled.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: if the GPS arming check is disabled, the fence must be disabled eventually.

property.property_specification: If the EKF failsafe is disabled, then the fence must be disabled.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: if the EKF failsafe is disabled, the fence must be disabled eventually.

property.property_specification: When the fence is enabled, the vehicle being armed implies that GPS lock is present.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that GPS lock must exist whenever the vehicle is armed with fence enabled

property.property_specification: If the fence is enabled, arming the vehicle eventually requires GPS lock to be acquired first.
property.reason: Expresses that enabling the fence leads to a state where arming requires GPS lock (though this is weaker than the invariant form)

property.property_specification: If the Fence is enabled, then GPS lock is required before arming the vehicle.
property.reason: Represents the 'leads to' relationship: if the fence is enabled, it will eventually lead to having GPS lock before arming.

property.property_specification: If EKF failsafe occurs, Fence is enabled, and GPS lock is lost while flying, then Fence will eventually be disabled.
property.reason: The conjunction of three conditions (EKF failsafe, Fence enabled, GPS lost) leads to the eventual disabling of the Fence. This is a 'leads to' temporal relationship.

property.property_specification: If EKF failsafe occurs and the Fence is enabled and you lose GPS lock while flying, then the Fence will eventually be disabled.
property.reason: The statement describes a leads-to relationship where a combination of conditions (EKF failsafe, Fence enabled, GPS loss while flying) leads to the eventual disabling of the Fence.

property.property_specification: If EKF failsafe occurs and the Fence is enabled and the vehicle is in an autonomous mode, it will eventually switch to LAND (or HOLD for Rover).
property.reason: Combination of conditions leads inevitably to LAND state (leads-to relationship).

property.property_specification: If EKF failsafe occurs and the Fence is enabled and the vehicle is in an autonomous mode, the vehicle will eventually switch to LAND mode.
property.reason: Expresses that if EKF failsafe occurs, the fence is enabled, and the mode is autonomous, then eventually the vehicle will switch to land mode.

property.property_specification: If EKF failsafe occurs and the Fence is enabled and the vehicle is in an autonomous mode, the vehicle will eventually switch to HOLD mode (for Rover).
property.reason: Expresses that if EKF failsafe occurs, the fence is enabled, and the mode is autonomous, then eventually the vehicle will switch to hold mode (for Rover).

property.property_specification: The pilot can retake control by switching to a manual mode, leading to manual mode.
property.reason: Manual mode switch action leads to manual state (leads-to relationship).

property.property_specification: Changing the flight mode leads to manual control.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is in this mode, it leads to a state where the pilot can re-take control after the vehicle is switched out of this mode.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle reaches either the polygon fence or cylindrical fence, then the failsafe behavior (stop or RTL) will eventually trigger
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where reaching any fence triggers failsafe behavior. The formula uses UPPAAL's --> operator to enforce that reaching either fence type (disjunction) inevitably causes the failsafe response.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle reaches the polygon fence first, the failsafe behavior will trigger.
property.reason: Expresses that when the polygon fence is reached, the failsafe behavior will be triggered using the leads to operator.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle reaches the cylindrical fence first, the failsafe behavior will trigger.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_TYPE is 2, inclusion zones overlap, a boundary is crossed, and FENCE_OPTIONS bit 1 is not set, then a fence breach will eventually occur.
property.reason: Captures the conditional leads-to relationship where crossing a boundary under specific fence configuration parameters (FENCE_TYPE=2, overlapping zones, and disabled union option) inevitably leads to a breach.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_TYPE = 2 and inclusion zones overlap, then crossing a boundary leads to a fence breach unless FENCE_OPTIONS bit 1 is set.

'overlap' and 'breach' are abstracted state variables, and FENCE_OPTIONS1 represents the state where bit 1 of FENCE_OPTIONS is set.

property.property_specification: If Rover reaches the Cylindrical fence boundary in Steering or Acro mode, it will eventually stop and not move beyond it.
property.reason: Leads-to relationship: Reaching the boundary in Steering/Acro mode inevitably leads to the 'stopped' state.

property.property_specification: If the rover is in Steering mode and reaches the cylindrical fence boundary, it will eventually stop.

property.property_specification: If the rover is in Acro mode and reaches the cylindrical fence boundary, it will eventually stop.

property.property_specification: If Rover reaches the Cylindrical fence boundary in modes other than Steering or Acro, it will eventually execute FENCE_ACTION.

property.property_specification: If the rover is not in Steering or Acro mode and reaches the cylindrical fence boundary, it will eventually execute the FENCE_ACTION.

property.property_specification: Whenever the copter is in Loiter, PosHold, or AltHold modes and altitude reaches FENCE_ALT_MAX, it does not increase altitude further.
property.uppaal_formula: A[] ((mode == Loiter || mode == PosHold || mode == AltHold) && altitude >= FENCE_ALT_MAX) imply (!increasing_altitude)
property.reason: Expresses an invariant (A[]) that in all states where the copter is in the specified modes and altitude is at/beyond the limit, altitude increase is prohibited.

property.property_specification: Whenever Copter is in Loiter, PosHold, or AltHold modes and altitude reaches FENCE_ALT_MAX, it is not increasing altitude.
property.uppaal_formula: A[] ((mode == Loiter || mode == PosHold || mode == AltHold) && (altitude >= FENCE_ALT_MAX) imply !increasing_alt)
property.reason: This is an invariant (A[]) ensuring that when the copter is in the specified modes and altitude reaches/exceeds FENCE_ALT_MAX, altitude increase is prohibited.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the copter's altitude will not increase further than FENCE_ALT_MAX in Loiter mode.
property.reason: Represents that in Loiter mode, the copter's altitude is inevitably less than or equal to FENCE_ALT_MAX.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the copter's altitude will not increase further than FENCE_ALT_MAX in PosHold mode.
property.reason: Represents that in PosHold mode, the copter's altitude is inevitably less than or equal to FENCE_ALT_MAX.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the copter's altitude will not increase further than FENCE_ALT_MAX in AltHold mode.
property.reason: Represents that in AltHold mode, the copter's altitude is inevitably less than or equal to FENCE_ALT_MAX.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in Loiter mode and exceeds the boundary, a breach is eventually declared.
property.reason: Leads-to relationship between exceeding boundary in Loiter mode and breach declaration.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in Loiter mode and exceeds the horizontal boundaries, it will eventually declare a breach.
property.reason: Expresses a "leads to" property: if the system is in 'Loiter' mode and exceeds the boundary, it will eventually lead to a 'breach' being declared.

property.property_specification: If the system is in a mode other than the current one and either the altitude is reached or the boundary is reached, then it will eventually execute FENCE_ACTION.
property.reason: The text describes a leads-to relationship: when in 'other modes' (modeled as a state 'other_mode') and the condition (altitude or boundary reached) holds, the system must eventually execute FENCE_ACTION (modeled as a state or action). This maps directly to the UPPAAL '-->' operator.

property.property_specification: If the altitude or boundary is reached, the FENCE_ACTION will eventually be executed.
property.reason: This describes a 'leads to' relationship. If 'altitude_reached' or 'boundary_reached' is true, then 'fence_action_executed' will eventually become true.

property.property_specification: Whenever Object Avoidance is enabled and RTL is active, the system eventually avoids fences.
property.reason: The statement implies a leads-to relationship where enabling Object Avoidance during RTL actions guarantees fence avoidance. The formula combines the two preconditions (ObjectAvoidanceSetup and RTL_in_progress) in the antecedent and the result (AvoidsFences) in the consequent using the UPPAAL leads-to operator.

property.property_specification: If Object Avoidance is setup, RTL actions will eventually avoid any fences as it returns.
property.reason: This extracts the 'leads to' relationship, where 'ObjectAvoidanceSetup' leads to 'avoid_fences'.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle's distance exceeds 100 meters, it will eventually switch to LAND/HOLD mode.
property.reason: Exceeding 100m triggers a transition to LAND/HOLD mode regardless of backup fences.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle's distance exceeds 20 meters and it is moving away from home, it will switch to LAND/HOLD mode.
property.reason: Exceeding 20m while moving away triggers LAND/HOLD mode as an additional backup fence.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle eventually proceeds 100m outside the configured fence distance, the vehicle will eventually switch into LAND mode.
property.reason: Expresses that if the vehicle is outside fence distance by 100m, it will eventually enter LAND mode.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is enabled and (fence boundaries are exceeded or altitude exceeds FENCE_ALT_MAX or falls below FENCE_ALT_MIN when these parameters are non-zero), the Plane will eventually execute FENCE_ACTION.
property.reason: This captures the 'leads to' relationship where exceeding fence/altitude constraints (with non-zero parameters) while enabled eventually triggers FENCE_ACTION. The condition combines enabled state, boundary violations, and altitude checks with parameter validity.

property.property_specification: If the fence is enabled and the fence boundaries or altitudes (FENCE_ALT_MAX and FENCE_ALT_MIN) are exceeded, then Plane will eventually execute the FENCE_ACTION.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If the fence is enabled and exceeded, eventually the fence action will be executed.

property.property_specification: The system can eventually reach the 'return point' state.
property.reason: The phrase 'Guided to return point' implies the possibility of reaching a specific state, which translates to a reachability property (E<>).

property.property_specification: Eventually, the system is at the return point.
property.reason: The phrase 'guided to return point' implies that eventually the system will reach or be at the 'return_point'.

property.property_specification: Whenever pilot throttle control is active, the system eventually reaches the return point
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: activation of pilot throttle control guarantees eventual arrival at the return point

property.property_specification: Eventually the system is at the return point with pilot throttle control.
property.reason: Expresses that 'return_point_with_pilot_throttle_control' will eventually (A<>) be true.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_ACTION is 6 or 7 and FENCE_RET_ALT is non-zero, altitude equals FENCE_RET_ALT.
property.reason: Leads-to relationship: Guided return mode with non-zero FENCE_RET_ALT inevitably sets altitude to FENCE_RET_ALT.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_ACTION is 6 or 7, FENCE_RET_ALT is zero, and altitude limits are non-zero, altitude equals midway between limits.
property.reason: Leads-to relationship: Guided return mode with zero FENCE_RET_ALT and non-zero limits inevitably sets altitude to midpoint.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_ACTION is 6 or 7, FENCE_RET_ALT is zero, and both altitude limits are zero, altitude equals current altitude.
property.uppaal_formula: A[] ((FENCE_ACTION == 6 || FENCE_ACTION == 7) && FENCE_RET_ALT == 0 && min_alt == 0 && max_alt == 0) --> (altitude == current_alt)
property.reason: Leads-to relationship: Guided return mode with zero FENCE_RET_ALT and zero limits inevitably retains current altitude.

property.property_specification: Mode changes are not allowed after a fence breach if FENCE_OPTIONS[0] is 1.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant: whenever a fence breach occurs and FENCE_OPTIONS[0] is 1, mode changes are prohibited.

property.property_specification: Mode changes cannot be executed after the fence breach unless the FENCE_OPTIONS bit 0 is set to “0”.
property.reason: This states that if a fence breach occurs and FENCE_OPTIONS bit 0 is 1, it is always the case that a mode change is not possible.

property.property_specification: If PWM is greater than 1800, all configured fences are enabled.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that whenever PWM exceeds 1800, fences must be enabled in all states along all paths.

property.property_specification: If PWM is less than 1800, all configured fences are disabled.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that whenever PWM is below 1800, fences must be disabled in all states along all paths.

property.property_specification: Whenever the PWM value is greater than 1800, all configured fences are enabled.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if PWM > 1800, then fences_enabled will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: If the system is on the ground, disarmed, and the Minimum Altitude fence is enabled, then a pre-arm failure will eventually occur.
property.reason: The text specifies a causal relationship where enabling the Minimum Altitude fence under the conditions of being on the ground and disarmed leads to a pre-arm failure. This corresponds to the UPPAAL 'leads to' operator (p --> q).

property.property_specification: If the Minimum Altitude fence is enabled by the switch while on the ground and disarmed, eventually a pre-arm failure will occur.
property.reason: This describes a leads-to relationship. If the Minimum Altitude fence is enabled, the system is on the ground, and disarmed, then a pre-arm failure will eventually occur.

property.property_specification: If FENCE_MARGIN is not loose enough, then altitude control imperfections will eventually lead to a breach.
property.reason: The statement expresses a 'leads to' relationship where insufficient FENCE_MARGIN (antecedent) inevitably causes a breach (consequent). This maps directly to UPPAAL's --> operator without nested temporal logic.

property.property_specification: If altitude control imperfections arise, and the FENCE_MARGIN parameter is not loose enough, then a breach will occur.
property.reason: Expresses that if altitude control imperfections exist and FENCE_MARGIN is not loose enough, breach will eventually occur.

property.property_specification: Stick mixing remains disabled while the plane is outside the fenced region.
property.reason: Ensures that stick mixing cannot be enabled until the plane is back inside.

property.property_specification: When the plane is outside the fence, stick mixing is disabled.
property.reason: The text specifies that stick mixing is disabled if the plane is outside the fence, forming an invariant that outside_fence implies ¬stick_mixing_enabled.

property.property_specification: When the plane is inside the fence, stick mixing is enabled.
property.reason: The text states that stick mixing is re-enabled as soon as the plane is inside the fence, implying an invariant that inside_fence always implies stick_mixing_enabled.

property.property_specification: Whenever the plane is inside the fence and stick mixing is enabled, if the plane goes outside the fence, stick mixing will eventually be disabled.
property.reason: An 'A[] (p imply A<> q)' relationship, expressed as 'p --> q' where p = 'insideFence && stickMixingEnabled' and q = '!stickMixingEnabled'. It describes a 'leads to' property. Whenever insideFence and stickMixingEnabled holds, eventually stickMixingEnabled will be false.

property.property_specification: Whenever the plane is outside the fence and stick mixing is disabled, if the plane goes inside the fence, stick mixing will eventually be enabled.
property.reason: An 'A[] (p imply A<> q)' relationship, expressed as 'p --> q' where p = '!insideFence && !stickMixingEnabled' and q = 'stickMixingEnabled'. It describes a 'leads to' property. Whenever !insideFence and !stickMixingEnabled holds, eventually stickMixingEnabled will be true.

property.property_specification: The system can eventually perform a failsafe action.
property.reason: Failsafe is listed as a common avoidance strategy, implying it is a reachable state.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the system will enter FailSafe mode.
property.reason: Expresses that the system inevitably enters FailSafe mode.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in AltHold or Loiter mode, horizontal object avoidance is active.
property.uppaal_formula: A[] ((mode == AltHold || mode == Loiter) imply horizontal_avoidance)
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that horizontal object avoidance must be enabled in AltHold/Loiter modes.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in Loiter or AltHold mode, upward object avoidance is active.
property.uppaal_formula: A[] ((mode == Loiter || mode == AltHold) imply upward_avoidance)
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that upward object avoidance must be enabled in Loiter/AltHold modes.

property.property_specification: If the copter is in ALTHOLD or LOITER mode and objects are detected in any quadrant, it will eventually move away from the threat or stop.
property.reason: The text describes a causal relationship where detection of objects in specific modes leads to a response (moving away or stopping). This matches the UPPAAL 'leads to' (-->) operator.

property.property_specification: If the copter is in ALTHOLD or LOITER mode, the system attempts to move away from the aggregate threat or stop.
property.reason: Expresses that when the system is in althold or loiter mode, it always leads to moving away or stopping to avoid a threat.

property.property_specification: Possibly, the Copter is in ALTHOLD mode and eventually collides with an object.
property.reason: The text states the pilot can override the system's safety measures in ALTHOLD mode, making collision reachable in some execution path.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the Copter may fly into an object.
property.reason: Expresses that there exists a path where the copter eventually flies into an object.

property.property_specification: Proximity-based avoidance is active if and only if the switch is HIGH.
property.reason: The text defines a direct correspondence between the switch state and the system state. When the switch is HIGH, ProximityAvoidanceActive is true; when not HIGH (LOW), it is false. This is a global invariant.

property.property_specification: If the switch is HIGH, proximity-based avoidance is on.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the switch is HIGH, then proximity avoidance is ON.

property.property_specification: If PRX1_TYPE is set to 4, the proximity sensor is enabled.
property.reason: The text specifies that setting PRX1_TYPE to 4 enables the proximity sensor. This is a 'leads to' relationship where the condition PRX1_TYPE == 4 eventually results in ProximityEnabled.

property.property_specification: Setting PRX1_TYPE to "4" enables the usage of the first range finder as a proximity sensor.
property.reason: This describes a 'leads to' relationship: setting PRX1_TYPE to 4 eventually leads to the proximity sensor being enabled.

property.property_specification: The system can possibly be in the Loiter state.
property.reason: Possibly entering the Loiter state.

property.property_specification: The system can possibly enter a loitering state.
property.reason: The term 'Loiter' implies a state that the system might eventually reach. The formula E<> Loitering captures the possibility of entering this state in some execution path.

property.property_specification: Rover always stops.
property.reason: The text explicitly states 'Rover always stops,' which translates to an invariant that Rover is always in the Stop state.

property.property_specification: Rover always stops.
property.reason: This statement indicates that the Rover's state is invariably 'stopped'. We represent it using the A[] (always) operator. 'Rover_state' is assumed to be a variable or expression that represents the state of Rover. 'stopped' is assumed to be a value that the state assumes.

property.property_specification: Whenever an obstacle approaches and the sensor detects it, the vehicle will eventually back away.
property.reason: The text specifies a leads-to relationship: detection of an approaching obstacle triggers the vehicle to eventually back away.

property.property_specification: If an obstacle approaches the vehicle from any direction and the sensor detects it, the vehicle will eventually back away from the obstacle.
property.reason: Expresses that if 'obstacle_approaches' and 'sensor_detects' are true, 'vehicle_backs_away' will eventually be true. It uses the 'leads to' operator.

property.property_specification: Possibly, when AVOID_ACCEL_MAX is too low, the vehicle does not stop in time.
property.reason: Expresses the risk that a low AVOID_ACCEL_MAX parameter may lead to a state where the vehicle fails to stop in time. The formula uses E<> to capture the existence of at least one path where both conditions hold.

property.property_specification: If AVOID_ACCEL_MAX is too low, the vehicle may not be able to stop in time.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship where a low AVOID_ACCEL_MAX leads to the vehicle not stopping in time.

property.property_specification: Possibly, when the sensor has a wide field of view during takeoff or landing, the ground is detected as an obstacle.
property.reason: The text states a possibility ('might see') under specific conditions (wide field of view during takeoff/landing). This corresponds to a reachability property (E<>) where the conjunction of these states holds.

property.property_specification: If the sensor has a wide field of view, the vehicle's takeoff eventually leads to the vehicle reacting to the ground as an obstacle.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the sensor has a wide field of view and the vehicle is taking off, eventually the vehicle will react to the ground as an obstacle.

property.property_specification: If the sensor has a wide field of view, the vehicle's landing eventually leads to the vehicle reacting to the ground as an obstacle.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the sensor has a wide field of view and the vehicle is landing, eventually the vehicle will react to the ground as an obstacle.

property.property_specification: Whenever the feature is working, there must be a valid Downward Facing Rangefinder reading
property.reason: The feature's operation is conditional on the presence of a valid reading, forming an invariant (always holds).

property.property_specification: If the feature is active, then a valid Downward Facing Rangefinder reading must eventually be available.
property.reason: Represents the requirement that if the feature is active it leads to eventually having a valid rangefinder reading.

property.property_specification: The vehicle always maintains a distance of at least OA_MARGIN_MAX (2m) from obstacles.
property.reason: The requirement specifies a safety invariant (always true) that the vehicle must stay at least OA_MARGIN_MAX (2m) away from obstacles. This translates directly to an invariant property in UPPAAL using the A[] operator.

property.property_specification: The vehicle maintains a distance to obstacles greater than or equal to OA_MARGIN_MAX.
property.reason: OA_MARGIN_MAX defines the minimum safe distance to obstacles, modeled as a system-wide invariant.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle's altitude above home is below OA_DB_ALT_MIN within a 3-meter radius around home, obstacles are rejected.
property.reason: The condition triggers an invariant rejection response. The formula enforces that whenever altitude and radius constraints are met, obstacle rejection occurs in all states (safety property).

property.property_specification: If the vehicle's altitude above home is below OA_DB_ALT_MIN within a 3-meter radius around home, OADatabase will reject obstacles.
property.reason: Expresses a condition that leads to a state (obstacles rejected) based on altitude and distance to home.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in Guided mode, the GUID_OPTIONS bit 6 must be set.
property.reason: The text specifies a mandatory condition (bit 6 must be set) when using Guided mode, which translates to a global invariant (A[]). The formula uses a logical implication to enforce the bit check whenever GuidedMode is active.

property.property_specification: If bit 7 of GUID_OPTIONS is set, it will be active in GUIDED mode.
property.reason: Models that if GUID_OPTIONS bit 7 is set, eventually the system will be in guided mode.

property.property_specification: If a proximity-based obstacle is detected while in Dijkstra's navigation mode, the system switches to BendyRuler navigation.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: detection of an obstacle during Dijkstra navigation eventually triggers a switch to BendyRuler.

property.property_specification: If the system starts with Dijkstra's navigation and a proximity-based obstacle is detected, then the navigation will switch to BendyRuler navigation.
property.reason: Expresses the transition from Dijkstra's navigation to BendyRuler upon obstacle detection as a leads-to relationship.

property.property_specification: If no obstacles are detected in the sensor's nearby view while in BendyRuler navigation mode, the system resumes Dijkstra's navigation.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: absence of obstacles during BendyRuler navigation eventually triggers a return to Dijkstra.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is using BendyRuler navigation and no obstacles are detected in the nearby view, then it will eventually switch back to Dijkstra's navigation.
property.reason: Expresses the transition from BendyRuler to Dijkstra navigation when no obstacles are detected as a leads-to relationship.

property.property_specification: Surface Tracking is possible when the system is in Loiter, PosHold, or AltHold modes.
property.reason: The text indicates Surface Tracking can be active (possibly) in these modes. E<> represents the possibility of being in SurfaceTracking while in one of the supported modes.

property.property_specification: Surface Tracking is possible in modes AltHold, Loiter, or PosHold.
property.uppaal_formula: E<> (SurfaceTracking && (mode == AltHold || mode == Loiter || mode == PosHold))
property.reason: The text states that Surface Tracking is supported in pilot-controlled modes like AltHold, Loiter, and PosHold. This implies there exists a path where SurfaceTracking is active while in one of these modes, captured by E<>.

property.property_specification: Whenever GCS provided terrain data is used, TERRAIN_ENABLE must be set to 1.
property.reason: The requirement enforces an invariant: if the system uses GCS terrain data (state 'using_GCS_terrain'), the parameter TERRAIN_ENABLE must always be 1.

property.property_specification: If the system is using GCS provided terrain data, the TERRAIN_ENABLE parameter is set to 1.
property.reason: Expresses the conditional relationship where if the system is using GCS terrain data, then the TERRAIN_ENABLE parameter will eventually be 1.

property.property_specification: Whenever the altitude type is set to Terrain, all mission commands with 'Alt' fields are interpreted as altitudes above terrain.
property.reason: This represents an invariant (A[]) where setting the altitude type to 'Terrain' implies that mission altitude fields are interpreted as above-terrain. The logical implication 'imply' enforces this relationship in all states.

property.property_specification: Once the altitude type is set to 'Terrain', all mission commands with 'Alt' fields will be interpreted as altitudes-above-terrain.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: once the altitude type is Terrain, mission commands' Alt fields will eventually be interpreted as altitudes-above-terrain.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in AUTO mode, the mission will eventually be executed
property.reason: The text implies that executing the mission is an inevitable outcome when in AUTO mode, requiring a leads-to relationship between the AUTO state and mission execution.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is in AUTO mode, a mission will eventually run.
property.reason: The text specifies that missions run when the vehicle switches to AUTO mode. This is a 'leads to' relationship (AUTO mode eventually triggers mission execution), represented by '-->' in UPPAAL.

property.property_specification: Eventually the mission is executed in AUTO mode.
property.reason: This asserts that in all possible executions, the mission will eventually be executed in AUTO mode. I'm inferring the existence of `mission_executed` and `mode` as system variables to represent the state of mission execution and vehicle mode, respectively.

property.property_specification: The parameter EK3_RNG_USE_HGT must always be -1.
property.reason: The text explicitly states that the parameter should remain at -1, which corresponds to an invariant (always true in all states).

property.property_specification: The parameter EK3_RNG_USE_HGT should always be equal to -1.
property.reason: Expresses that EK3_RNG_USE_HGT should always have the value -1 (invariant).

property.property_specification: Whenever no area is selected, the system eventually uses the actual displayed area.
property.reason: The text describes a conditional behavior: the absence of a selected area triggers the use of the displayed area. This corresponds to a 'leads to' relationship (p --> q) in UPPAAL, ensuring that if 'no_area_selected' holds, 'using_displayed_area' will eventually hold.

property.property_specification: If no area is selected, the actual displayed area is used.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: If no area is selected (noAreaSelected), then the actual displayed area is used (displayedArea) eventually.

property.property_specification: If Digital Elevation Model files are loaded into Mission Planner, then the generated terrain file will use them as a source.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where loading DEM files causes the terrain file to use them.

property.property_specification: If Digital Elevation Model files are loaded into Mission Planner, then the generated terrain file will eventually use them as a source.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: loading DEM files eventually leads to them being used as a source in the generated terrain file.

property.property_specification: A terrain data file is always created as one file per one-by-one degree area.
property.reason: The phrase 'always created as one file per one-by-one degree area' implies an invariant (A[]) that whenever a file is created (file_created), it must satisfy the one-per-degree condition (one_file_per_degree_area).

property.property_specification: Terrain data files are always created.
property.reason: Expresses that 'terrain_data_file_created' always holds (invariant).

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is connected to a compatible GCS, the terrain data will eventually not be missing (as it is automatically re-downloaded).
property.reason: Expresses that connection to GCS leads to eventual resolution of missing terrain data (automated re-download).

property.property_specification: Eventually, the terrain data will be re-downloaded when connected to a compatible GCS.
property.reason: Expresses that being connected to a GCS leads to terrain data being downloaded.

property.property_specification: When upgraded to 4.1.x with GCS connected to the Internet and loaded missions/home location, tiles are eventually re-downloaded.
property.reason: Captures the 'leads to' relationship where connectivity and mission/home conditions trigger automatic tile re-download after upgrade.

property.property_specification: If upgrading from 4.0.x to 4.1.x, eventually the tiles on the SD card will be re-downloaded when the GCS is connected to the Internet for areas covered by loaded missions and/or home location.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship where upgrading and GCS being connected implies tiles are re-downloaded eventually.

property.property_specification: When upgraded to 4.1.x without Internet connection and TERRAIN_MARGIN=50, old tileset remains in use.
property.reason: Represents the alternative path where manual configuration preserves old tileset when offline.

property.property_specification: If upgrading from 4.0.x to 4.1.x and the GCS is not connected to the Internet, eventually terrain_margin is set to 50 implies the old tileset can be used.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship where upgrading and GCS not connected and margin set implies old tileset being used.

property.property_specification: If terrain data is unavailable during RTL with TERRAIN_FOLLOW enabled, altitude interpretation defaults to home.
property.reason: Handles edge case where missing terrain data forces fallback to home altitude as invariant.

property.property_specification: When terrain data is unavailable, RTL_ALT is interpreted as altitude-above-home, regardless of the TERRAIN_FOLLOW setting.
property.reason: The system enforces the RTL_ALT interpretation as altitude-above-home under the condition of missing terrain data, independent of TERRAIN_FOLLOW. This is modeled as a conditional 'leads to' property.

property.property_specification: If terrain follow is not set and the system does not have immediate access to terrain data, then RTL interprets RTL_ALT as an altitude above home.
property.reason: Expresses the condition under which RTL interprets RTL_ALT as altitude above home using the leads-to operator.

property.property_specification: If during RTL, WPNAV_RFND_USE is 1, RTL_ALT is within the rangefinder's operating range, and the rangefinder is healthy, then the rangefinder is used instead of the terrain database.
property.reason: The 'leads to' (-->) operator captures the requirement that whenever the combined condition holds (RTL mode, WPNAV_RFND_USE enabled, valid RTL_ALT, and healthy rangefinder), the system must eventually use the rangefinder instead of the terrain database.

property.property_specification: When WPNAV_RFND_USE is 1, and RTL_ALT is within the rangefinder's operating range, and the rangefinder is healthy, then the rangefinder will be used instead of the terrain database during RTL.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If WPNAV_RFND_USE is 1, RTL_ALT is within the rangefinder's operating range, and the rangefinder is healthy, then the rangefinder is used during RTL.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is executing a mission requiring terrain data, unable to retrieve terrain data for two seconds, and is flying, it will eventually switch to RTL mode.
property.reason: The formula captures the requirement that when the mission is active, terrain data retrieval fails for 2 seconds (clock x), and the vehicle is flying, it must eventually enter RTL mode. The 'leads to' operator (-->) enforces the causal relationship.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is executing a mission command that requires terrain data and the vehicle is flying and it is unable to retrieve terrain data for two seconds, the vehicle will switch to RTL mode.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: if the vehicle is executing a mission command that requires terrain data, it is flying, terrain data is unavailable, and the terrain data has been unavailable for 2 seconds (x >= 2), then it will switch to RTL mode.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is executing a mission requiring terrain data, unable to retrieve terrain data for two seconds, and is landed, it will eventually disarm.
property.reason: The formula captures the requirement that when the mission is active, terrain data retrieval fails for 2 seconds (clock x), and the vehicle is landed, it must eventually disarm. The 'leads to' operator (-->) ensures the outcome.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is executing a mission command that requires terrain data and the vehicle is landed and it is unable to retrieve terrain data for two seconds, the vehicle will disarm.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: if the vehicle is executing a mission command that requires terrain data, it is landed, terrain data is unavailable, and the terrain data has been unavailable for 2 seconds (x >= 2), then it will disarm.

property.property_specification: If the ground station does not have terrain data at the requested resolution, it eventually interpolates to provide the grid size.
property.reason: The text describes a conditional leads-to relationship: absence of terrain data (p = !terrain_data_available) triggers interpolation (q = interpolating). This aligns with UPPAAL's 'p --> q' syntax, which enforces that whenever p holds, q will eventually hold.

property.property_specification: If the ground station does not have terrain data available at the resolution requested by the aircraft then the ground station will eventually provide the requested grid size.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If the ground station lacks terrain data (noTerrainData), it will eventually provide the requested grid size (provideGridSize).

property.property_specification: Configuring the auxiliary switch to turn on the rangefinder leads to the rangefinder eventually being on.
property.reason: The text specifies the auxiliary switch can be configured to enable the rangefinder. This corresponds to a leads-to relationship where activating the switch causes eventual activation of the rangefinder.

property.property_specification: It is possible to configure a switch to turn on the rangefinder.
property.reason: Expresses the possibility of the rangefinder being turned on.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is out of the rangefinder's range, the pilot's stick directly controls the target climb rate.
property.reason: This expresses an invariant that when the 'out_of_range' condition holds, the control mode must be 'direct'.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle climbs out of the rangefinder’s range, the pilot’s stick returns to directly controlling the vehicle’s target climb rate.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. `outOfRange` represents the system state where the vehicle is out of the rangefinder's range, and `stickControlsClimbRate` represents the system state where the pilot's stick directly controls the climb rate.

property.property_specification: Whenever inverted flight is enabled (RCx_OPTION switch 43 activated), the vehicle eventually stabilizes with a roll of 180 degrees.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: Enabling inversion (invert_enabled) inevitably results in stabilization at 180-degree roll (roll == 180).

property.property_specification: Whenever inverted flight is enabled, the vehicle will stabilize with a roll of 180 degrees from normal.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: if inverted flight is enabled, the vehicle will eventually have a roll of 180 degrees.

property.property_specification: Parameters are adjusted only if the vehicle's actual thrust curve has been measured.
property.reason: The text specifies a logical constraint that parameter adjustment (adjusted) must only occur if the thrust curve measurement (thrust_measured) precondition is satisfied. This maps directly to an invariant implication in UPPAAL.

property.property_specification: Normally, the parameters should not be adjusted unless the vehicle’s actual thrust curve has been measured.
property.reason: This statement means that if the thrust curve has been measured (measured), then eventually the parameters might be adjusted (adjusted). This is captured with the leads to operator.

property.property_specification: Enabling Harmonic Notch filters leads to higher low-pass frequency and tighter tune.
property.reason: Notch filters enable subsequent parameter adjustments that improve tuning.

property.property_specification: Enabling either one or both of the Harmonic Notch filters eventually allows a tighter tune.
property.reason: Expresses a leads to relationship based on the text. 'harmonicNotchEnabled' represents the state where either one or both Harmonic Notch filters are enabled, and 'tighterTune' represents the state where a tighter tune is achieved.

property.property_specification: After rebooting, the relevant parameters will eventually appear.
property.reason: The text specifies that parameters become visible after a reboot, which corresponds to a 'leads to' relationship (rebooted eventually leads to parameters_visible).

property.property_specification: Eventually, all the relevant parameters will appear after rebooting.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: after rebooting, it leads to a state where relevant parameters appear.

property.property_specification: Upon rebooting, the system will eventually use the parameter values.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where rebooting will lead to the system using the parameter values eventually.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTCH_OPTS (or INS_HNTC2_OPTS if the second set of notches is enabled) has bit 1 set, then a set of notches for each motor will be created.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that when bit 1 is set in the relevant options, per-motor notches are active. Assumes INS_HNTCH_OPTS is the primary option (second set disabled).

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTCH_OPTS has bit 1 set, then a set of notches for each motor will be created.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: If bit 1 of INS_HNTCH_OPTS is set (equal to 1), then 'notches_created' will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTC2_OPTS has bit 1 set and the second set of notches is enabled, then a set of notches for each motor will be created.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship with two conditions: If bit 1 of INS_HNTC2_OPTS is set and the second set is enabled, then 'notches_created' will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTCH_OPTS does not have bit 1 set (and INS_HNTC2_OPTS is not applicable), the average frequency of all motors will set the center frequency.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that when bit 1 is not set in the primary option (INS_HNTCH_OPTS), the system defaults to average frequency. Assumes second set is disabled.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTCH_OPTS does not has bit 1 set, then the average frequency of all motors will set the center frequency.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: If bit 1 of INS_HNTCH_OPTS is not set (equal to 0), then 'center_frequency_set' will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTC2_OPTS does not has bit 1 set or the second set of notches is disabled, then the average frequency of all motors will set the center frequency.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship with two conditions: If bit 1 of INS_HNTC2_OPTS is not set or the second set is disabled, then 'center_frequency_set' will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: Enabling more harmonic notch filters than required eventually leads to running out of CPU cycles.
property.reason: The causal relationship 'enabling too many will result in [...]' is modeled as a leads-to (-->) property, where exceeding the required filters inevitably causes CPU exhaustion.

property.property_specification: Enabling too many harmonic notch filters will always result in running out of CPU cycles.
property.reason: Expresses that a high number of enabled filters always implies the CPU running out of resources. 'num_filters_enabled > threshold' represents 'enabling too many harmonic notch filters' and 'cpu_cycles_remaining <= 0' represents 'running out of CPU cycles'.

property.property_specification: Enabling too many harmonic notch filters leads to running out of CPU cycles.
property.reason: Expresses that exceeding a limit in the number of enabled filters eventually leads to the CPU running out of resources. 'num_filters_enabled > threshold' represents 'enabling too many harmonic notch filters' and 'cpu_cycles_remaining <= 0' represents 'running out of CPU cycles'.

property.property_specification: If the system is in FFT Mode, then the three largest noise sources will eventually have a notch assigned.

property.property_specification: If using FFT Mode, eventually the three largest noise sources will have a notch assigned.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if in FFT Mode, then eventually notches are assigned to the three largest noise sources.

property.property_specification: If the system is in ESC Telemetry Mode, then each motor will eventually have a notch assigned at its RPM.
property.reason: Translates a conditional (ESC Telemetry Mode) leading to an eventual outcome (notch assignment per motor RPM) using the 'leads to' operator.

property.property_specification: If ESC Telemetry Mode is active, eventually each motor will have a notch assigned at its RPM.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if in ESC Telemetry Mode, then eventually each motor has a notch assigned at its RPM.

property.property_specification: If the system is in Throttle Mode, then each motor will eventually have a notch assigned at its throttle input.
property.reason: Translates a conditional (Throttle Mode) leading to an eventual outcome (notch assignment per motor throttle input) using the 'leads to' operator.

property.property_specification: If throttle mode is active, eventually each motor will have a notch assigned at its throttle input.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: under throttle mode, each motor eventually has a notch assigned at its throttle input.

property.property_specification: If any RPM source (Sensor, ESC telem, FFT) fails, the system will eventually use the minimum frequency (FFT_MINHZ, INS_HNTCH_FREQ, or INS_HNTC2_FREQ) for the notch center frequency.
property.reason: The statement expresses a 'leads to' relationship between RPM source failure (antecedent) and minimum frequency usage (consequent), which maps directly to UPPAAL's --> operator.

property.property_specification: If the RPM source fails, then the minimum frequency will be used for the notch center frequency.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: If RPM source fails, then eventually the minimum frequency is used.

property.property_specification: If the autopilot is capable of running FFT (CapableFFT), and FFT_ENABLE is enabled, and RC Auxiliary function 162 is used (RC_Aux_162_Used) during sustained hover (Hovering), then the parameters (INS_HNTCH_FREQ, INS_HNTCH_ENABLE, INS_HNTCH_MODE) will eventually be automatically set (ParametersSet).
property.reason: The text describes a conditional 'leads to' relationship: specific preconditions (autopilot capability, enabling flags, and hover state) must lead to eventual parameter configuration. This maps directly to UPPAAL's '-->' operator.

property.property_specification: If the autopilot is capable of running the FFT and FFT_ENABLE is enabled by using RC Auxiliary function 162 during a sustained hover, then INS_HNTCH_FREQ, INS_HNTCH_ENABLE, and INS_HNTCH_MODE will automatically be setup.

property.property_specification: If the harmonic notch is enabled (enabled == 1), then either INS_HNTCH_ENABLE or INS_HNTC2_ENABLE must be set to 1.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is 1 or INS_HNTC2_ENABLE is 1, then the harmonic notch is enabled.
property.reason: The text specifies a logical condition where enabling either/both variables results in the harmonic notch being active. This translates to an invariant (A[]) asserting that whenever the variables are set to 1, the system state reflects the harmonic notch is enabled.

property.property_specification: Whenever INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is 1, the harmonic notch is enabled
property.reason: The formula enforces that whenever the variable INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is set to 1, the system must ensure the harmonic notch is enabled (invariant). This directly maps the enabling condition described in the text.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is 1, then eventually the harmonic notch is enabled.
property.reason: Expresses that when INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is 1, the harmonic notch enabled state will eventually be reached.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTC2_ENABLE is 1, then eventually the harmonic notch is enabled.
property.reason: Expresses that when INS_HNTC2_ENABLE is 1, the harmonic notch enabled state will eventually be reached.

property.property_specification: Setting INS_HNTCH_ENABLE to 1 implies the harmonic notch is enabled.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship: If INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is 1, then harmonic_notch_enabled will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: Setting INS_HNTC2_ENABLE to 1 implies the harmonic notch is enabled.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship: If INS_HNTC2_ENABLE is 1, then harmonic_notch_enabled will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: When INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is 1, the harmonic notch is enabled.
property.reason: Expresses that if INS_HNTCH_ENABLE is 1, then harmonicNotchEnabled will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: If INS_HNTCH_OPTS or INS_HNTC2_OPTS bit 1 is set, the bandwidth ratio must be 4/1 instead of the default 2/1.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: setting either bit requires the bandwidth ratio to change from default (2/1) to 4/1.

property.property_specification: If bit 1 of INS_HNTCH_OPTS or INS_HNTC2_OPTS is set, the bandwidth must not be in the default ratio of 2/1.
property.reason: Invariant requiring bandwidth ratio to deviate from default when specific bits are set.

property.property_specification: If bit 1 of INS_HNTCH_OPTS or INS_HNTC2_OPTS is set, the bandwidth should not be half the frequency.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the specified bit is set, the bandwidth will eventually not be half the frequency.

property.property_specification: If bit 1 of INS_HNTC2_OPTS is set, the bandwidth should not be half the frequency.
