property.property_specification: If the battery and USB cable are disconnected, then the autopilot will eventually power down
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship where disconnecting both power sources (battery and USB) guarantees the autopilot eventually powers down. Uses UPPAAL's --> operator to enforce causal dependency between preconditions and outcome.

property.property_specification: If the battery and USB cable are disconnected, then the autopilot will eventually power down.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: disconnection of battery and USB leads to autopilot power down.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle has a buzzer attached, then the arming tone will eventually be played
property.reason: The conditional relationship (buzzer existence implies eventual arming tone) matches UPPAAL's leads-to operator (p --> q), where p is the condition and q is the guaranteed eventual outcome.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle has a buzzer attached, the arming tone will be played.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the vehicle has a buzzer, then eventually the arming tone will be played

property.property_specification: Pressing the safety button eventually leads to the autopilot displaying solid red
property.reason: The text implies a causal relationship where pressing the button (trigger) leads to the display turning solid red (outcome), captured by the 'leads to' (-->) operator.

property.property_specification: The autopilot will eventually display solid red after the safety button is pressed
property.reason: The phrase 'until it displays solid red' implies inevitability of the red display state after button interaction, requiring the 'A<>' operator for global eventual occurrence.

property.property_specification: Whenever the autopilot has a safety switch, pushing the switch leads to the LED eventually displaying solid red.
property.reason: This expresses a 'leads to' relationship: pushing the safety switch on an autopilot with a safety switch activated leads to the LED eventually showing solid red.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the autopilot displays solid red after pressing the safety button.
property.reason: Expresses the requirement that after the safety button is pressed, the autopilot will eventually display solid red.

property.property_specification: If motors are not all in the 'running' state or their speed difference exceeds a threshold, then ESCs are not calibrated.
property.reason: This captures the leads-to relationship between failure conditions (desynchronized start/speed) and the ESC calibration status.

property.property_specification: If the motors do not all start at the same time and spin at the same speed, the ESC’s are still not properly calibrated.
property.reason: Expresses a leads to relationship. If motors don't start at the same time and don't spin at the same speed, then ESCs are not calibrated.

property.property_specification: If the autopilot is in All-at-once mode and powered up, then all ESCs receive the same PWM signal (equal to the pilot's throttle).
property.uppaal_formula: A[] (Autopilot.Mode == AllAtOnce && poweredUp imply ESC.PWM == PilotThrottle)
property.reason: The All-at-once mode directly passes the pilot's throttle to ESCs when powered up, ensuring the same PWM signal is sent.

property.property_specification: If the autopilot is powered up in all-at-once ESC Calibration mode, it will send the same PWM signal to all ESCs.
property.reason: Expresses that the all-at-once ESC calibration mode leads to sending the same PWM signal.

property.property_specification: If the system is powered up with full throttle, the ESC enters programming mode.
property.reason: Full throttle at startup triggers the ESC to enter programming mode (as per common ESC behavior).

property.property_specification: While the ESC is in programming mode, the upper endpoint is set to the full throttle position.
property.reason: Full throttle at programming mode initialization defines the upper endpoint.

property.property_specification: Whenever the motor range is set, ESC calibration must have been completed.
property.reason: The text specifies a strict ordering: ESC calibration must be done before setting the motor range. This is a safety property ensuring that in all states globally (A[]), if the motor range is set (MotorRangeSet), calibration (CalibrationComplete) must already hold.

property.property_specification: If ESC calibration is completed, then motor range setting will eventually be completed.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: completion of ESC calibration leads to eventual completion of motor range setting.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is armed and not flying, the motors spin at MOT_SPIN_ARM speed.
property.reason: Invariant ensuring motor speed configuration in armed/non-flying state.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is armed but not flying, the motors will spin.
property.reason: Expresses that if the vehicle is armed and not flying, it leads to the motors spinning.

property.property_specification: MOT_SPIN_MIN must always be at least 0.03 higher than MOT_SPIN_ARM
property.reason: The text defines a static invariant constraint between two parameters, requiring MOT_SPIN_MIN to maintain a minimum offset of 0.03 above MOT_SPIN_ARM in all system states.

property.property_specification: MOT_SPIN_ARM is always greater than MOT_SPIN_MIN
property.reason: The condition MOT_SPIN_ARM > MOT_SPIN_MIN is stated as a persistent requirement, implying it must hold invariantly (always true in all states).

property.property_specification: MOT_SPIN_ARM is too high or MOT_SPIN_MIN is too low
property.reason: Represents the logical disjunction of two conditions.

property.property_specification: MOT_SPIN_MIN parameter value is too high
property.reason: This statement directly represents a system state related to the MOT_SPIN_MIN parameter. It can be treated as a boolean variable or a state in UPPAAL.

property.property_specification: If ESC RPM telemetry is active and one or more motors are not spinning (Motor_Spinning == false), the system must never attempt takeoff (Takeoff_Attempted == false).
property.reason: Encodes the prevention of takeoff attempts under motor failure conditions when ESC RPM telemetry is configured. Combines the parameter constraint and motor state to enforce safety.

property.property_specification: If ESC RPM telemetry is being used and one or more motors are not spinning, then takeoff attempts will eventually be prevented.
property.reason: The condition 'ESC RPM telemetry is being used and one or more motors are not spinning' leads to 'takeoff attempts will eventually be prevented'.

property.property_specification: Once the vehicle enters a failsafe mode (Radio/Battery/GCS/Terrain Data Loss) involving a mode change, it remains in that mode until the pilot directly changes the mode.
property.reason: This captures the requirement that being in FailsafeMode implies it persists until PilotChangeMode occurs. However, this formula is a tautology (FailsafeMode → FailsafeMode ∨ ...) and does not enforce the intended behavior due to UPPAAL syntax limitations. The correct temporal constraint cannot be expressed without nesting operators.

property.property_specification: If any failsafe (Radio Failsafe, Battery Failsafe, GCS Failsafe, or Terrain Data Loss Failsafe) is entered and the action involves a mode change, the vehicle will remain in that mode until the pilot changes it directly.
property.reason: Expresses that if a failsafe is triggered and involves a mode change, the vehicle will remain in that mode until the pilot changes it. 'failsafeEntered' represents entering any of the listed failsafe states. 'modeChangeAction' represents the condition where a mode change occurs due to the failsafe. 'pilotChangeMode' represents the pilot directly changing the mode of the vehicle; it is the inevitable result of the mode change action.

property.property_specification: If the transmitter signal is lost and (the receiver sends no signal or does not hold RC channel 8 value), then the helicopter will eventually crash.
property.reason: The condition (signal loss + invalid channel 8 behavior) leads inevitably to crash. This matches UPPAAL's leads-to (p --> q) semantics.

property.property_specification: If the RC receiver loses connection to the transmitter and is not configured correctly, the motor will shutdown and the helicopter will crash.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship where loss of connection with improper configuration inevitably causes motor shutdown and crash.

property.property_specification: If the receiver loses connection to the transmitter and the receiver is not configured correctly, the helicopter will crash.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: If receiver loses connection AND receiver is not configured correctly, then the helicopter will crash eventually.

property.property_specification: If the radio failsafe is enabled and set up correctly, then whenever any condition occurs for more than RC_FS_TIMEOUT seconds, the failsafe will eventually trigger.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where enabled/setup state combined with persistent conditions (≥RC_FS_TIMEOUT) eventually triggers failsafe. Uses clock 't' for time constraints and disjunction for 'any condition'.

property.property_specification: If the radio failsafe is enabled and set-up correctly, then if certain conditions occur for more than RC_FS_TIMEOUT seconds, the radio failsafe will trigger.
property.reason: The natural language describes a leads-to relationship. If "enabled and setup_correctly and condition" is true, then "x > RC_FS_TIMEOUT", where x represents elapsed time, will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: It is possible that the RC transmitter is eventually turned off.
property.reason: The pilot's action of turning off the RC transmitter implies a reachable state where 'RC_Transmitter_Off' holds. This corresponds to the 'possibly' (E<>) temporal operator.

property.property_specification: The RC transmitter is eventually turned off.
property.reason: Expresses that it is possible to reach a state where the RC transmitter is off using E<>.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the transmitter is off.
property.reason: Expresses that on all paths, the transmitter will eventually be in the 'off' state.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is outside RC range, the signal is lost.
property.reason: The statement describes an invariant relationship where the vehicle being outside RC range implies the signal is lost. This is directly expressible as an invariant (A[]) with a logical implication between two system states.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the vehicle will travel outside of the RC range, and the signal will be lost.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship. If a vehicle goes out of range, it leads to a lost signal.

property.property_specification: The throttle channel can eventually be forced below FS_THR_VALUE by the pilot via the transmitter.
property.reason: The text implies the pilot can force the throttle channel into a state below a specific threshold, which corresponds to a reachability property (possibly). The formula E<> checks if this state is achievable.

property.property_specification: The pilot forces the throttle channel below FS_THR_VALUE from the transmitter.
property.reason: This describes a system state where the throttle value is below a threshold.

property.property_specification: If the system is using GCS only, then RC_OVERRIDES will eventually be lost.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where the condition 'GCS_only' being true causes 'RC_OVERRIDES_lost' to eventually occur, using UPPAAL's --> operator.

property.property_specification: If a GCS only is being used, RC_OVERRIDES are eventually lost.
property.reason: This states that if 'gcs_only' is in use, then 'lost_overrides' will eventually hold. I have used a leads to relationship between the two states.

property.property_specification: After a radio failsafe is triggered, it is possible for the copter to do nothing.
property.reason: The copter can be configured to 'do nothing' as a possible response to failsafe, implying reachability.

property.property_specification: If the radio failsafe is triggered, eventually the copter may do nothing.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship: if the radio failsafe is triggered, the copter may do nothing.

property.property_specification: After a radio failsafe is triggered, it is possible for the copter to land immediately.
property.reason: The copter can be configured to 'land' as a possible response to failsafe, implying reachability.

property.property_specification: If a radio failsafe is triggered while already in a landing phase, the copter will continue landing.
property.reason: Conditional behavior: failsafe during landing phase leads to continued landing.

property.property_specification: If the radio failsafe is triggered, eventually the copter may land immediately.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship: if the radio failsafe is triggered, the copter may land immediately.

property.property_specification: Eventually, when a GCS failsafe is triggered, the copter will land immediately.
property.reason: Expresses the conditional relationship where triggering the GCS failsafe leads to the copter landing immediately.

property.property_specification: After a radio failsafe is triggered, it is possible for the copter to execute RTL.
property.reason: The copter can be configured to 'RTL' as a possible response to failsafe, implying reachability.

property.property_specification: After a radio failsafe is triggered, it is possible for the copter to execute SmartRTL.
property.reason: The copter can be configured to 'SmartRTL' as a possible response to failsafe, implying reachability.

property.property_specification: If the radio failsafe is triggered, eventually the copter may RTL.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship: if the radio failsafe is triggered, the copter may RTL.

property.property_specification: If the radio failsafe is triggered, eventually the copter may SmartRTL.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship: if the radio failsafe is triggered, the copter may SmartRTL.

property.property_specification: Always, if the copter is disarmed, then failsafe does not occur.
property.reason: The statement specifies an invariant that whenever the copter is disarmed (state condition), the failsafe cannot be active (safety property). This aligns with UPPAAL's A[] operator for global invariants.

property.property_specification: If the copter is disarmed, no failsafe will take place.
property.reason: Representing the 'leads to' relationship: if 'disarmed' holds, then 'failsafe' will not eventually hold. Note: Here, 'disarmed' represents a system state where the drone is disarmed, and 'failsafe' represents a system state where failsafe is active.

property.property_specification: Whenever the copter is armed and in Stabilize or Acro mode and throttle input is at minimum, it eventually disarms.
property.reason: The statement describes a conditional leads-to relationship: the conjunction of being armed, in specific modes, and throttle at minimum implies eventual disarm. This aligns with UPPAAL's '-->' operator (A[](p imply A<> q)).

property.property_specification: If the copter is armed in Stabilize or Acro modes, and the throttle input is at minimum, the copter will eventually disarm.
property.reason: The text describes a 'leads to' relationship. If the copter is armed in Stabilize or Acro modes, and the throttle input is at minimum, the copter will eventually disarm. This can be represented using the '-->' operator in UPPAAL.

property.property_specification: Whenever the transmitter and receiver regain contact (failsafe clears), the copter remains in RTL mode.
property.reason: The formula enforces that whenever contact is regained (contact_regained), the copter stays in RTL mode (invariant).

property.property_specification: When the failsafe clears (GCS reconnected and MAVLink heartbeat restored), the copter remains in RTL mode.
property.reason: Captures the invariant that after failsafe clearance, the mode remains RTL until manual intervention.

property.property_specification: The copter will eventually transition to Loiter mode only if the pilot changes the flight mode switch to another position and back to Loiter.
property.reason: This 'leads to' formula captures that after contact is regained and the copter is in RTL, it will transition to Loiter mode only if the pilot explicitly switches modes (implied via model transitions, not directly expressible here).

property.property_specification: If the pilot changes the flight mode switch to Loiter after switching to another position, the vehicle's mode will eventually be Loiter.
property.reason: Represents the required pilot action (switch cycle) to regain Loiter mode. Assumes a state transition where the switch leaves and returns to Loiter.

property.property_specification: If contact with the transmitter is lost, low-end receivers hold all channels at their last known position.
property.reason: Describes an alternative bad behavior where channel states are frozen upon contact loss.

property.property_specification: If contact with the transmitter is lost, some low end receivers will hold all channels at their last known position.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship. If 'lostContact' holds, then eventually 'heldChannels' will hold.

property.property_specification: If RTL is enabled (Value 1) and GPS is usable, the copter will eventually enter RTL Mode.
property.reason: RTL being enabled with usable GPS leads to RTL Mode. The formula uses 'leads to' (-->) to enforce that the condition (RTL_enabled and GPS_usable) eventually results in RTL_Mode.

property.property_specification: If RTL is enabled (Value 1) and GPS is not usable, the copter will eventually enter Land Mode.
property.reason: RTL being enabled with unusable GPS leads to Land Mode. The formula uses 'leads to' (-->) to enforce that the condition (RTL_enabled and !GPS_usable) eventually results in Land_Mode.

property.property_specification: If SmartRTL or RTL is enabled (Value 4) and SmartRTL is available and GPS is usable, the copter will eventually enter SmartRTL mode.
property.reason: Represents the conditional transition to SmartRTL mode when Value 4 is enabled, SmartRTL is available, and GPS is functional.

property.property_specification: If SmartRTL or RTL is enabled (Value 4) and SmartRTL is not available and GPS is usable, the copter will eventually enter RTL mode.
property.reason: Encodes the fallback to RTL mode when Value 4 is enabled but SmartRTL is unavailable (with valid GPS).

property.property_specification: If Enabled Always RTL (Value 1), then eventually the copter will be in RTL Mode.
property.reason: Expresses that if the 'EnabledAlwaysRTL' condition is met, then the 'RTLEnabled' state will eventually be reached.

property.property_specification: If Enabled Always RTL (Value 1) and the GPS position is not usable, then eventually the copter will be in Land Mode.
property.reason: Expresses that if 'EnabledAlwaysRTL' is true and 'GPSUsable' is false, the 'LandMode' state will eventually be reached.

property.property_specification: Enabling Always Land (Value 3) leads to the copter eventually switching to Land Mode.
property.reason: The text describes a causal relationship: enabling Always Land (modeled as a condition like 'LandEnabled == 3') triggers a transition to Land Mode. This matches the 'leads to' (-->) operator in UPPAAL, which enforces that whenever the antecedent holds, the consequent eventually holds.

property.property_specification: If 'Enabled Always Land' (Value 3) is true, the copter will eventually switch to Land Mode.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship where the condition 'EnabledAlwaysLand3' leads to the state 'LandMode'.

property.property_specification: If 'Enabled Always Land' has value 5, then the copter will eventually be in 'Land Mode'.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If EnabledAlwaysLand equals 5, the system will eventually reach the state LandMode.

property.property_specification: If SmartRTL or RTL is enabled (Value 4) and GPS is not usable, the copter will eventually enter Land mode.
property.reason: Captures the transition to Land mode when GPS fails, overriding other mode selections under Value 4.

property.property_specification: If Enabled SmartRTL or Land (Value 5) is active and SmartRTL is not available, the copter will eventually switch to Land Mode.
property.reason: The text specifies a conditional leads-to relationship where Enabled state without SmartRTL availability results in Land mode.

property.property_specification: If GPS is unusable, the copter eventually enters Land mode.
property.reason: Leads-to relationship: GPS failure triggers transition to Land mode.

property.property_specification: If SmartRTL is not available, the copter will eventually be in Land mode.
property.reason: Expresses that if SmartRTL is not available, the copter will eventually be in Land mode.

property.property_specification: If Enabled SmartRTL or Land (Value 4), then the copter will switch to SmartRTL mode.
property.reason: This represents the condition that when SmartRTL or Land is enabled, the system will eventually enter the SmartRTL mode.

property.property_specification: If SmartRTL is not available, then the copter will switch to Land Mode instead.
property.reason: This represents the condition that when SmartRTL is not available, the system will eventually enter the Land mode.

property.property_specification: If Enabled SmartRTL or Land (Value 5) is active and SmartRTL is available, the copter will eventually switch to SmartRTL mode.
property.reason: The text specifies a conditional leads-to relationship where Enabled state with SmartRTL availability results in SmartRTL mode.

property.property_specification: When SmartRTL or RTL (Value 3) is enabled, the copter eventually enters SmartRTL mode.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: enabling SmartRTL/RTL triggers a transition to SmartRTL mode.

property.property_specification: If Enabled SmartRTL or Land, the copter will eventually be in SmartRTL mode.
property.reason: Expresses that if SmartRTL or Land is enabled, the copter will eventually be in SmartRTL mode.

property.property_specification: Whenever Auto DO_LAND_START or RTL is enabled, the system eventually transitions to DO_LAND_START or RTL.
property.reason: The text describes a 'leads to' relationship where enabling Auto (condition) eventually results in entering either DO_LAND_START or RTL (target states). The UPPAAL '-->' operator directly encodes this liveness property.

property.property_specification: If 'Auto DO_LAND_START or RTL' is enabled, then the system will eventually execute 'DO_LAND_START' or 'RTL'.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship between the 'enabled' state and the eventual execution of either 'DO_LAND_START' or 'RTL'.

property.property_specification: Whenever GPS position is not available, the system will eventually transition to BRAKE or LAND state.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship where the absence of GPS (condition) necessitates eventual activation of BRAKE/LAND (outcome). The '-->' operator captures the implication with eventual enforcement.

property.property_specification: If enabled and GPS position is not available, then eventually either Brake or Land will be in the BRAKE or LAND state.
property.reason: Expresses that if 'Enabled' is true and 'GPS_position' is false, then eventually (leads to) either 'Brake' or 'Land' will be true. Assumes 'Brake' and 'Land' represent states.

property.property_specification: Whenever the transmitter is off and the throttle stick is fully down, FS_THR_VALUE must be at least 10 PWM higher than Channel 3's PWM value.
property.reason: This is an invariant requiring FS_THR_VALUE to satisfy a condition when specific states (transmitter_off and throttle_down) hold.

property.property_specification: Whenever the transmitter is on and the throttle stick is fully down, FS_THR_VALUE must be at least 10 PWM lower than Channel 3's PWM value.
property.reason: This is an invariant requiring FS_THR_VALUE to satisfy another condition under different states (transmitter_on and throttle_down).

property.property_specification: If the transmitter is off, then eventually the throttle PWM value will be at least 10 below the FS PWM field value.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where turning off the transmitter (antecedent) guarantees the throttle PWM eventually drops to the required threshold (consequent). Both conditions are simple expressions without nested temporal operators.

property.property_specification: Whenever the throttle stick is fully down and the transmitter is off with Channel 3's PWM value set, the FS_THR_VALUE parameter must be at least 10 PWM higher than Channel 3's PWM value.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where if the throttle is down and the transmitter is off, the FS_THR_VALUE should eventually be greater than or equal to the channel 3 PWM value plus 10.

property.property_specification: Whenever the throttle stick is fully down and the transmitter is on with Channel 3's PWM value set, the FS_THR_VALUE parameter must be at least 10 PWM lower than Channel 3's PWM value.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where if the throttle is down and the transmitter is on, the FS_THR_VALUE should eventually be less than or equal to the channel 3 PWM value minus 10.

property.property_specification: If the transmitter is off, then the throttle PWM value will eventually be at least 10 below the FS PWM field value.
property.reason: This expresses a leads-to relationship. if transmitter_off, then eventually the throttle_pwm will be less than or equal to the FS PWM field value minus 10.

property.property_specification: FS_THR_VALUE must always be above 910 PWM.
property.reason: This is a global invariant requiring FS_THR_VALUE to remain above 910 PWM in all states.

property.property_specification: The FS_THR_VALUE parameter must always be above 910 PWM.
property.reason: Expresses that the FS_THR_VALUE parameter is always greater than 910.

property.property_specification: If the system is in auto mode and Radio Failsafe is active, then it will eventually continue.
property.reason: The statement expresses a leads-to relationship where the conjunction of being in auto mode and Radio Failsafe activation leads to the system eventually continuing. This maps directly to UPPAAL's leads-to (-->) operator between two state/condition expressions.

property.property_specification: If the system is in guided mode and Radio Failsafe is active, and bit 2 is set, then the system will eventually continue.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where the conjunction of guided_mode, radio_failsafe, and bit2_set states eventually leads to the system continuing.

property.property_specification: If the system is in auto mode during Radio Failsafe, then continue.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if in auto mode and radio failsafe occurs, then the system will continue. 'auto', 'radioFailsafe', and 'continue' are system states.

property.property_specification: If the system is in auto mode and Ground Control Station failsafe is active, then the system continues.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if 'auto' mode and 'gcs_failsafe' hold, then 'continue' will eventually hold.

property.property_specification: If bit 1 is set and the system is in auto mode during Ground Control Station Failsafe, then the system continues (remains in auto mode).
property.uppaal_formula: A[] ((bit1_set && Failsafe && SystemMode == Auto) --> SystemMode == Auto)
property.reason: The formula enforces that whenever bit1 is set, the system is in auto mode, and a failsafe occurs, the system remains in auto mode. This directly translates the 'continue' requirement into an invariant property.

property.property_specification: Whenever bit 1 is set and the Ground Control Station Failsafe is active, the system must be in auto mode.
property.reason: This captures the conditional requirement that the system must be in auto mode during failsafe if bit1 is set, ensuring the specified behavior is enforced globally.

property.property_specification: If none of the above options are set, then FS_THR_ENABLE will eventually be executed.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where the absence of prior options triggers the execution of FS_THR_ENABLE. The formula uses UPPAAL's --> operator to enforce that the condition 'none_of_above_set' (e.g., a boolean state) inevitably leads to 'FS_THR_ENABLE_executed' (a target state).

property.property_specification: If none of the above are set, then the FS_THR_ENABLE option will eventually be executed.
property.reason: Expresses that if a combined condition (none of the above) is met, then FS_THR_ENABLE will eventually be executed. This is modeled using the 'leads to' operator.

property.property_specification: If none of the above are set, then eventually FS_GCS_ENABLE is executed.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if 'none_set' is true, then 'FS_GCS_ENABLE_executed' will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: If the system is using Low-Throttle and loses radio contact, then the throttle channel must eventually drop.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship where the condition (Low-Throttle method active and radio contact lost) eventually triggers the throttle channel drop.

property.property_specification: If the "Low-Throttle" method is used, then the throttle channel drops with loss of radio contact.
property.reason: This expresses a leads-to relationship: if LowThrottleUsed is true, then ThrottleDropped will eventually be true.

property.property_specification: The throttle PWM value is always at least 10 higher than the FS PWM field value.
property.reason: The text explicitly states the invariant requirement that the throttle PWM must 'definitely be at least 10 higher' than the FS PWM value, which maps to an A[] temporal operator to enforce this condition globally.

property.property_specification: The throttle PWM value should eventually be at least 10 higher than the value held in the FS PWM field.
property.reason: Expresses that eventually, the throttlePWM should be at least 10 greater than fsPWM.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in STABILIZE or ACRO mode and throttle is zero, the motors eventually disarm.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where the condition (STABILIZE/ACRO mode and zero throttle) must eventually result in motors disarming.

property.property_specification: When the transmitter is turned off while in stabilize mode with motors armed and throttle at zero, the motors will eventually disarm.
property.reason: The text specifies a causal relationship where turning off the transmitter under specific conditions (stabilize mode, armed motors, zero throttle) leads to eventual motor disarming. This matches the UPPAAL 'leads to' (-->) operator semantics.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is in Stabilize or Acro mode with throttle at zero or the vehicle is landed, the motors are disarmed.
property.uppaal_formula: A[] (((mode == Stabilize || mode == Acro) && (throttle == 0)) || landed) -> motors_disarmed
property.reason: The formula encodes the invariant that if either (Stabilize/Acro mode with zero throttle) OR (landed) holds, then motors_disarmed must hold in all states.

property.property_specification: Lowering throttle to zero leads to motors being disarmed
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship between throttle lowering action and motor disarming state

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in a relevant mode and the throttle has been at minimum for 15 seconds, the motors will eventually disarm.
property.reason: The statement describes a 'leads to' relationship where maintaining throttle_min for 15 seconds in a relevant mode inevitably causes disarm. Clock 'x' tracks the duration of throttle_min.

property.property_specification: If the system is in STABILIZE mode with throttle at zero, the motors will eventually disarm.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship; if in STABILIZE mode and throttle is zero, then eventually motors will be disarmed.

property.property_specification: If the system is in ACRO mode with throttle at zero, the motors will eventually disarm.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship; if in ACRO mode and throttle is zero, then eventually motors will be disarmed.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is in Stabilize mode and the throttle is at zero, then the motors are disarmed eventually.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the vehicle is in Stabilize mode and the throttle is zero, it leads to motors being disarmed.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is in Acro mode and the throttle is at zero, then the motors are disarmed eventually.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the vehicle is in Acro mode and the throttle is zero, it leads to motors being disarmed.

property.property_specification: Whenever the throttle is above zero, the flight mode eventually changes to RTL or LAND.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the system retakes control after the failsafe has cleared.

property.property_specification: Whenever the flight mode is changed to Stabilize (after being in RTL/LAND and armed), the Failsafe page display must eventually show Stabilize.

property.reason: Ensures that switching to Stabilize mode (from RTL/LAND while armed) leads to the display updating to Stabilize.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in stabilize mode, the motors must be armed and the throttle must be above zero.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in stabilize mode and the motors are armed, the throttle should eventually be above zero.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship between being in stabilize mode and having the motors armed, and the throttle being above zero.

property.property_specification: The system must eventually switch Flight Mode to RTL or LAND

property.property_specification: The Flight Mode switching to RTL leads to the Flight Mode eventually switching to LAND.
property.reason: This captures the 'leads to' relationship. If the Flight Mode becomes RTL, it will eventually become LAND.

property.property_specification: Whenever the receiver's power is reattached (on), the autopilot must already be powered down.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle battery voltage drops below a specified voltage for 10 seconds, the vehicle eventually switches to RTL, SmartRTL, or Land mode.

property.reason: The voltage condition requires a time constraint (10 seconds) and triggers a mode switch. The formula uses a clock variable 'x' to model the duration and the 'leads to' operator to enforce the transition.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle battery voltage drops below a specified voltage for 10 seconds, the vehicle will eventually be in RTL, SmartRTL or Land mode.

It states that if the system is in specific modes and GPS fails or glitches for 5 seconds, it will lead to a failsafe event. 'time' is a clock variable that measures the duration of the GPS failure or glitches. The clock 'time' must be reset when the copter is not experiencing a GPS disturbance. 'failsafe' is a boolean variable.

property.property_specification: If the roll stick is moved right (clockwise) while moving counterclockwise and the speed reaches zero, the system will stop.
property.reason: Expresses that when the speed is zero, the system will eventually stop.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is stopped (speed 0) and the roll stick is pushed in any direction (left/right), it will eventually start moving in that direction.
property.reason: After stopping, pushing the stick in a direction restarts movement. This maps to a 'leads to' relationship between the stopped state with stick input and movement resumption.

property.property_specification: Whenever the speed is zero, pushing the roll stick in either direction will eventually lead to movement in the desired direction.
property.reason: Captures the "leads to" relationship where zero speed eventually leads to a moving state when the roll stick is used.

property.property_specification: Whenever CH6 tuning knob is set for circle rate, roll stick rate changes are inhibited.
property.reason: The statement describes a safety invariant (A[]) where the condition CH6 knob being set to circle rate implies inhibition of roll stick rate changes. This matches UPPAAL's A[] operator for invariants.

property.property_specification: If CH6 tuning knob is set for circle rate, then roll stick rate changes are inhibited.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If the CH6 tuning knob is set for circle rate (CH6_tuning_knob_circle_rate), it leads to the roll stick rate changes being inhibited (roll_stick_rate_changes_inhibited).

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in radio failsafe mode, stick changes are inhibited.
property.reason: The statement asserts an invariant that in all states (A[]), if the system is in radio failsafe mode (radio_failsafe), then stick changes must be inhibited (stick_changes_inhibited). This matches the A[] operator for invariants.

property.property_specification: In radio failsafe, stick changes are inhibited.
property.reason: Expresses that if the system is in 'radio_failsafe', then 'inhibited' will eventually be true. Here, 'inhibited' represents the state where stick changes are inhibited.

property.property_specification: When bit 2 of CIRCLE_OPTIONS is set, circle center is set to current location upon mode entry.
property.reason: Bit 2 defines invariant center position when mode is active.

property.property_specification: When bit 2 of CIRCLE_OPTIONS is not set, circle center is set to perimeter-front position upon mode entry.
property.reason: Bit 2 being unset defines alternative invariant center position.

property.property_specification: When bit 3 of CIRCLE_OPTIONS is set, the mount faces the circle center at all times.
property.reason: Bit 3 enforces invariant mount orientation toward center.

property.property_specification: If bit 3 of CIRCLE_OPTIONS is set, the mount's ROI will be set on the circle center, causing the mount to always face the circle's center.
property.reason: This states that if a condition (bit 3 of CIRCLE_OPTIONS being set) holds, then a property (mount always faces the circle center) will eventually hold. This can be represented by the leads-to operator.

property.property_specification: Whenever the LOITER_TURNS command is active, the system eventually enters Circle mode.
property.reason: The text describes a causal relationship where LOITER_TURNS command execution leads to the system entering Circle mode. This corresponds to the UPPAAL 'leads to' operator (-->) where the precondition (LOITER_TURNS) implies the eventual postcondition (Circle_mode).

property.property_specification: The mission command LOITER_TURNS invokes Circle mode eventually.
property.reason: This indicates that when the 'LOITER_TURNS' command is active, the system will eventually enter 'Circle' mode.

property.property_specification: Whenever the right stick is pushed forward or back, the copter will eventually pitch and accelerate in the appropriate direction.
property.reason: The text describes a causal relationship where pushing the stick (forward/back) leads to pitching and acceleration. This is mapped to the UPPAAL 'leads to' operator (-->), enforcing that the action (stick input) eventually triggers the effect (pitching + acceleration).

property.property_specification: If the right stick is pushed to the right, the copter will eventually turn right.
property.reason: The action of pushing the stick right leads to the copter turning right, captured by the 'leads to' operator.

property.property_specification: When the right stick is pushed forward or back, the copter will eventually pitch in the appropriate direction.
property.reason: This states that if the right stick is pushed (forward or back), the copter will eventually pitch. The 'leads to' operator (-->) expresses this relationship.

property.property_specification: Whenever the copter is in Drift Mode and the right stick is centered, it will eventually drift (due to wind).
property.reason: The text states that under Drift Mode with centered stick, the copter 'will slowly drift in the wind.' This implies a 'leads to' relationship where the initial condition (DriftMode && right_stick_center) inevitably results in drifting over time.

property.property_specification: If the copter is in Drift Mode and the right stick is in the center, the copter will hold horizontal position loosely.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If the copter is in DriftMode and RightStickCenter, then HorizontalPositionLoosely will eventually hold.

property.property_specification: If the throttle is completely down, the motors will eventually enter the MOT_SPIN_ARMED state.
property.reason: Direct mapping of the conditional relationship between throttle input and motor state.

property.property_specification: If the pilot puts the throttle completely down, the motors will eventually go to MOT_SPIN_ARMED.
property.reason: Direct translation of the conditional relationship between throttle down and motor state.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is flying and the throttle is completely down, it will eventually lose attitude control and tumble.
property.reason: Combined condition for vehicle state transition when throttle is down during flight.

property.property_specification: If the pilot puts the throttle completely down and the vehicle is flying, the vehicle will eventually tumble.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: If throttle is down and the vehicle is flying, it eventually tumbles.

property.property_specification: If the pilot puts the throttle completely down and the vehicle is flying, it will lose attitude control and tumble.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship. If the throttle is completely down and the vehicle is flying, it eventually leads to a loss of attitude control and tumbling.

property.property_specification: If the copter is in Drift Mode and loses GPS signal, it will eventually land or enter altitude hold based on the failsafe_gps_enabled setting.
property.reason: The system behavior implies a 'leads to' relationship: losing GPS in Drift Mode eventually results in either Landing or AltitudeHold, depending on a configuration parameter (failsafe_gps_enabled). The disjunction captures the two possible outcomes controlled by the setting.

property.property_specification: If the GPS signal is lost in flight while in Drift Mode, then either the copter will land or the copter will enter altitude hold.
property.reason: Expresses the condition that if gps is lost and the copter is in drift mode, it will lead to either landing or altitude hold.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle is rising for at least 1 second, it will eventually flip.
property.reason: Expresses that rising for 1 second (clock constraint) leads to flipping.

property.property_specification: Eventually the vehicle will flip.
property.reason: Expresses that the 'flipped' state will eventually be reached along all paths.

property.property_specification: After the switch is brought high, the vehicle can flip again.
property.reason: Allows a new flip only after the switch is cycled (low then high).

property.property_specification: If the flip switch is high, then eventually the vehicle will flip.
property.reason: Expresses that if the 'flip_switch_high' state holds, then eventually the 'flipped' state will hold.

property.property_specification: If roll stick is held left, the vehicle will eventually flip left.
property.reason: Captures the 'leads to' relationship between roll-left input and left flip.

property.property_specification: If roll or pitch stick is held slightly left, forward or back the vehicle will eventually flip in that direction.
property.reason: Expresses the eventual flipping in the specified direction when the stick is held.

property.property_specification: If pitch stick is held forward, the vehicle will eventually flip forward.
property.reason: Captures the 'leads to' relationship between pitch-forward input and forward flip.

property.property_specification: If pitch stick is held back, the vehicle will eventually flip back.
property.reason: Captures the 'leads to' relationship between pitch-back input and back flip.

property.property_specification: Whenever the copter's orientation is between -90 degrees and +45 degrees, it must be rolling right at 400 degrees per second and have reduced throttle.
property.reason: Specifies an invariant (A[]) that whenever the orientation is within the given range, the system must enforce the roll rate and throttle reduction.

property.property_specification: If the copter is between +45 degrees and -90 degrees, then roll right at 400 degrees/sec and reduce throttle.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If the copter angle is between +45 and -90 degrees (copter_angle_45_to_neg90), it leads to rolling right at 400 deg/sec (roll_right_400) and reducing throttle (reduce_throttle).

property.property_specification: If the system is in Loiter mode and a rangefinder is attached, then high performance will eventually be achieved.
property.reason: Expresses that using Loiter mode with a rangefinder leads to high performance (temporal implication).

property.property_specification: If a rangefinder is attached, the system will eventually enter Loiter mode.
property.reason: Expresses the relationship between attaching a rangefinder and eventually entering Loiter mode.

property.property_specification: Whenever the user releases the sticks, the autopilot will eventually use the optical flow sensor to bring the vehicle to a stop.
property.reason: The release of sticks (condition) leads to the autopilot using the sensor (outcome), forming a 'leads to' temporal relationship.

property.property_specification: When the user releases the sticks, the optical flow sensor is used by the autopilot to bring the vehicle to a stop.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. When 'releasedSticks' becomes true, 'vehicleStopped' will eventually become true.

property.property_specification: If the flow sensor quality falls below FHLD_QUAL_MIN, FlowHold will eventually behave like AltHold
property.reason: The text specifies a conditional leads-to relationship: a low flow quality triggers a state where FlowHold acts like AltHold. This maps directly to UPPAAL's '-->' operator, enforcing that whenever the condition (flow_quality < FHLD_QUAL_MIN) holds, AltHold behavior must eventually follow.

property.property_specification: If the flow sensor quality falls below FHLD_QUAL_MIN, then FlowHold will act like AltHold eventually.
property.reason: Expresses that when flow sensor quality is below the minimum, FlowHold leads to acting like AltHold.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is in Follow mode, it is following another vehicle at a specified offset.
property.reason: The text states that entering Follow mode triggers the vehicle to follow another vehicle. This is an invariant property ensuring that Follow mode implies the vehicle is in a following state.

property.property_specification: When the vehicle is in Follow mode, it will eventually attempt to follow another vehicle.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship: if the vehicle is in 'Follow' mode, then eventually 'follow_attempted' will be true.

property.property_specification: When FOLL_ENABLE is 1, follow mode is active and parameters are refreshed.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that whenever FOLL_ENABLE equals 1, both 'follow_mode' and 'params_refreshed' states must hold.

property.property_specification: If FOLL_ENABLE is set to 1, then follow mode is enabled.
property.reason: Expresses that if FOLL_ENABLE is 1, then follow mode is enabled.

property.property_specification: FOLL_OFS_TYPE must be 0 when offsets are North-East-Down
property.reason: The system defines FOLL_OFS_TYPE as 0 for North-East-Down offsets, implying this value must invariantly hold in that configuration.

property.property_specification: If FOLL_OFS_TYPE is set to 0, then offsets are North-East-Down.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if a variable is 0, a certain state is reached.

property.property_specification: FOLL_OFS_TYPE must be 1 when offsets are relative to lead vehicle’s heading
property.reason: The system defines FOLL_OFS_TYPE as 1 for lead vehicle’s heading offsets, implying this value must invariantly hold in that configuration.

property.property_specification: If FOLL_OFS_TYPE is set to 1, then offsets are relative to the lead vehicle's heading.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if a variable is 1, a certain state is reached.

property.property_specification: It is possible for the follower vehicle to be in a state where it points in the same direction as the lead vehicle.
property.reason: The text explicitly states one controllable behavior is following the lead's direction. This corresponds to a reachability property (E<>) for the 'same_direction' state.

property.property_specification: It is possible for the follower vehicle to be in a state where it always points towards the lead vehicle.
property.reason: The text explicitly states another controllable behavior is always pointing towards the lead. This corresponds to a reachability property (E<>) for the 'always_towards' state.

property.property_specification: Whenever bit 0 of FOLL_OPTIONS is set to 1, the Gimbal/Mount Controls are enabled to follow the target vehicle.
property.reason: The formula expresses an invariant (A[]) that whenever bit 0 is set (FOLL_OPTIONS & 1 != 0), the system state 'follow_enabled' (Gimbal/Mount Controls enabled) must hold. This directly maps the configuration rule to a UPPAAL invariant.

property.property_specification: Setting bit 0 to 1 enables Gimbal/Mount Controls to follow the target vehicle.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: If bit 0 is set to 1, then Gimbal/Mount Controls will eventually follow the target vehicle.

property.property_specification: After takeoff, the drone will eventually switch to Loiter mode.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: once takeoff occurs, the system must eventually reach the Loiter state.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the drone will be in Loiter mode.
property.reason: Expresses that Loiter will eventually be reached from any initial state.

property.property_specification: If 'Fly to Here' works (FlyToHereSuccess), then eventually the system is ready for Follow Me mode (FollowMeReady).
property.reason: The text describes a conditional progression: success in 'Fly to Here' leads to readiness for Follow Me mode. This corresponds to the UPPAAL 'leads to' operator (-->), where FlyToHereSuccess triggers eventual FollowMeReady.

property.property_specification: If the right-click "Fly to Here" function works, then eventually the system will be in Follow Me mode.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the 'FlyToHereWorks' state is true, then eventually the 'FollowMeMode' state will be true.

property.property_specification: Whenever the user is moving, the Copter eventually follows.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where the user's movement triggers the Copter's following behavior eventually.

property.property_specification: Eventually the Copter follows you.
property.reason: The Copter should follow you eventually.

property.property_specification: When the copter reaches the target location, it will eventually hover.
property.reason: Reaching the target location triggers a guaranteed transition to a hovering state.

property.property_specification: Whenever a target location is set, the copter will eventually reach it.
property.reason: Setting a target location guarantees eventual arrival, forming a leads-to relationship.

property.property_specification: Once the location is reached, the copter will hover at that location.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the location is reached, the copter will eventually hover. I have 'locationReached' and 'hovering' as abstract system states.

property.property_specification: If the system is navigating via waypoints, it will eventually reach the target position.
property.reason: The text implies that waypoint navigation leads to reaching the target position. The 'leads to' relationship is captured by the UPPAAL --> operator.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the system will approach each waypoint.
property.reason: Expresses the inevitable arrival at waypoints.

property.property_specification: If Bit 0 is set (1), arming in Guided mode via the RC transmitter is allowed.
property.reason: The statement expresses a 'leads to' relationship where enabling Bit 0 allows arming in Guided mode. This maps directly to UPPAAL's 'p --> q' syntax, where p is the enabling condition (bit0_set) and q is the resulting state (armed_guided).

property.property_specification: If bit 0 is 1, then arming in Guided mode from the RC transmitter is allowed.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship. If bit 0 is 1, it leads to the arming in Guided mode being allowed.

property.property_specification: Whenever Bit2 is active, the pilot cannot change the vehicle's heading via the RC transmitter.
property.reason: Expresses an invariant that Bit2 being true globally prevents the 'can_change_heading' state.

property.property_specification: If bit 2 is set (e.g., to 4), the pilot cannot change the vehicle's heading using the RC transmitter.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if bit2 is set, it leads to a state where the pilot cannot change heading.

property.property_specification: Whenever Bit 4 is set, the position controller’s XY axis position error correction is disabled.
property.reason: Direct invariant relationship: Bit4 being set (bit4_set) implies correction is disabled (correction_disabled) in all states.

property.property_specification: If bit 4 is set, XY axis position error correction is disabled.
property.reason: This expresses that when bit4 is set to 1, it leads to XY axis position correction being disabled (XY_axis_correction == 0).

property.property_specification: If Bit 7 is enabled (e.g., set to 128), then weathervaning will eventually be active.
property.reason: The statement implies a causal relationship where enabling Bit 7 leads to weathervaning being activated. This corresponds to UPPAAL's 'leads to' operator (-->) to enforce that whenever Bit 7 is enabled, weathervaning must eventually occur.

property.property_specification: If bit 7 is set, weathervaning is enabled.
property.reason: Describes a leads-to relationship. If bit7_set then eventually weathervaning_enabled.

property.property_specification: If the helicopter loses propulsive power, it will eventually land safely.
property.reason: The text states that autorotation allows safe landing after power loss. This corresponds to a 'leads to' relationship where power loss (trigger) eventually results in safe landing (outcome).

property.property_specification: The primary goal of the manoeuvre is that eventually the helicopter lands safely.
property.reason: Expresses that eventually the helicopter will land safely along all paths.

property.property_specification: If there is insufficient power, the helicopter will eventually descend.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where insufficient power inevitably causes descent.

property.property_specification: Without sufficient power to maintain flight, a helicopter will descend.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if there is no power, the helicopter will eventually descend.

property.property_specification: Whenever a failure is initiated from a low airspeed state, the forward airspeed must be maintained or increased.
property.reason: The text specifies a safety invariant: if the failure occurs from a low airspeed state, the airspeed must be maintained or increased in all subsequent states. This translates directly to an A[] formula with a logical implication in the state condition.

property.property_specification: If the failure is initiated from a low airspeed state, the forward airspeed must be increased.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: Initiating a failure from a low airspeed state leads to an increase in airspeed.

property.property_specification: If a failure happens, the forward airspeed must be maintained.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. A failure leads to maintaining the current airspeed.

property.property_specification: If turning to land into wind is possible, the aircraft will eventually do so.
property.reason: Encodes the conditional 'where possible' as a leads-to relationship between the enabling condition and the outcome.

property.property_specification: Whenever the aircraft is in the specified phase of flight, it eventually turns to land into the wind, if possible.
property.reason: The text indicates that during a particular phase of flight, the aircraft will 'turn to land into wind' if the turn is feasible. This can be represented as a leads-to relationship.

property.property_specification: The aircraft must be landed before the rotor head speed slows too much, resulting in blade stall; blade stall must never occur.
property.reason: The text explicitly states that blade stall must be avoided by landing before rotor speed drops critically. This corresponds to a safety invariant prohibiting blade_stall.

property.property_specification: The aircraft must be landed before the head speed slows too much, resulting in blade stall.
property.reason: If the head speed slows too much (head_speed_low), then the aircraft must be landed (landed).

property.property_specification: When exiting the autonomous autorotation flight mode, the aircraft will eventually enter the non-manual collective mode.
property.reason: The text specifies a 'leads to' relationship: exiting autonomous autorotation (trigger) eventually results in entering non-manual collective mode (outcome). This aligns with UPPAAL's '-->' operator semantics.

property.property_specification: When exiting the autonomous autorotation flight mode, the aircraft will eventually be in a "non-manual collective mode".
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship between exiting autorotation and entering a non-manual collective mode.

property.property_specification: Whenever the bail-out phase is initiated, it will eventually reach a state where the vertical speed matches the stick's requested magnitude after the set time period AROT_BAIL_TIME.
property.reason: The text specifies that after the set time (AROT_BAIL_TIME), vertical speed matches the stick input. This is a leads-to relationship between phase activation and outcome.

property.property_specification: Initiation of the bail-out phase leads to having the requested vertical speed match the magnitude requested on the stick.
property.reason: Expresses that when the bailout is initiated, the vertical speed will eventually match the stick magnitude.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in bail-out, the ramp time is one second.
property.reason: The system enforces a hard-coded ramp time of 1 second during bail-out, overriding H_RSC_RAMP_TIME. This is an invariant property.

property.property_specification: During bail-out, the motor ramp time is one second.
property.reason: This indicates a leads-to relationship: if the system is in 'bailout' state, then the 'motor_ramp_time_equals_one' state will eventually be reached.

property.property_specification: The ramp time (H_RSC_RAMP_TIME) is active only when the aircraft is on ground and the landed flag is true.
property.reason: Specifies an invariant condition where the ramp time activation depends on two simultaneous states: aircraft being grounded and the landed flag being true.

property.property_specification: Whenever the aircraft is on the ground and the landed flag is true, the ramp time defined by H_RSC_RAMP_TIME is observed.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: being on the ground with the landed flag set implies the ramp time is observed.

property.property_specification: Once the mode has been sufficiently tested, the final instructions will be published and maintained on the wiki page.
property.reason: The text describes a 'leads to' relationship: sufficient testing eventually results in instructions being published. This aligns with UPPAAL's '-->' operator semantics (A[](p imply A<> q)).

property.property_specification: Once the mode has been sufficiently tested, final instructions will eventually be published.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship: If the 'tested' state is reached, eventually 'published' will hold.

property.property_specification: When exiting autorotation from auto mission, enter Altitude Hold.
property.reason: Expresses leads-to relationship for safety-driven mode transition exception

property.property_specification: When exiting autorotation, if the previous mode was an auto mission, the mode is exited into Altitude Hold.
property.reason: Expresses the leads-to relationship of exiting to altitude hold if previous mode was auto mission.

property.property_specification: If the copter is on the ground and the pilot's throttle is at minimum, then the motors will eventually shut down and the copter will disarm.
property.reason: The text specifies a conditional leads-to relationship: reaching the ground combined with minimum throttle inevitably causes shutdown and disarming. This aligns with UPPAAL's '-->' operator, which enforces that whenever the premise holds, the conclusion must eventually hold.

property.property_specification: If the copter reaches the ground and the pilot's throttle is at minimum, the copter will eventually shut down the motors and disarm itself.
property.reason: This expresses a 'leads to' relationship: when the copter is on the ground and the throttle is at minimum, the motors will eventually be off and the copter disarmed.  This uses states: ground, throttle_min, motors_off, and disarmed.

property.property_specification: Copter recognizes landing when motors are commanded low, climb rate is between -20cm/s and +20cm/s, no acceleration for 1 second, and altitude <10m (or <2m with rangefinder).
property.reason: Combines all landing detection conditions (motor state, climb rate, acceleration duration, and altitude constraints) into a leads-to relationship.

property.property_specification: If the motors are commanded to be at low level by the vertical position controller, its climb rate remains between -20cm/s and +20cm/s, and is not accelerating for one second, then the copter will recognize that it has landed.
property.reason: This states that if motors are low, climb rate is okay, and there is no acceleration, then the copter will eventually have landed. I have abstracted 'motors at low level', 'climb rate between -20cm/s and +20cm/s', 'no acceleration for one second', and 'copter landed' to MotorsLow, ClimbRateOK, NoAcceleration, and Landed respectively.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle does not have GPS lock, the horizontal control mode is stabilize.
property.reason: The text specifies an invariant relationship where the absence of GPS lock (gps_lock == false) directly implies the horizontal control mode is stabilize. This is encoded as an invariant (A[]) with a logical implication between the two states.

property.property_specification: If the vehicle does not have GPS lock, then the horizontal control will be in stabilize mode.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship. If GPS lock is not present (NoGpsLock), eventually the horizontal control enters StabilizeMode.

property.property_specification: If the copter is in Alt Hold, Loiter, Auto, AutoLand, or RTL mode, near the ground, and the barometer is affected by prop-wash, then its operation becomes erratic.
property.uppaal_formula: ((AltHold || Loiter || Auto || AutoLand || RTL) && near_ground && barometer_affected) --> erratic_operation
property.reason: Translates the conditional relationship where specific modes, proximity to ground, and barometer interference lead to erratic operation.

property.property_specification: If the copter's operation becomes erratic when close to the ground or landing in AltHold, Loiter, Auto, AutoLand, or RTL modes, it is probable that prop-wash is affecting the barometer.
property.uppaal_formula: erratic && (mode_AltHold || mode_Loiter || mode_Auto || mode_AutoLand || mode_RTL) --> propwash_affects_barometer
property.reason: The statement describes a leads-to relationship: if some modes are activiated and operation becomes erratic, then it leads to propwash affecting barometer.

property.property_specification: If an auto landing procedure is active and the barometer is affected by prop-wash, then bouncing or failure to turn off motors after landing occurs.
property.reason: Captures the specific consequence of barometer interference during auto landing, leading to bouncing or motor failure.

property.property_specification: If any auto landing procedure results in bouncing or failure to turn off motors after landing, prop-wash is probably affecting the barometer.
property.reason: The statement describes a leads-to relationship: if auto landing results in specific failures, it leads to propwash affecting barometer.

property.property_specification: If there is a good GPS lock, low magnetic interference, and low vibrations, then good loiter performance will eventually be achieved.
property.reason: Expresses a 'leads to' relationship where the conjunction of three conditions (simple expressions) eventually results in good loiter performance. The UPPAAL '-->' operator directly encodes this implication.

property.property_specification: Good loiter performance implies the system has good GPS position, low magnetic interference, and low vibrations.
property.reason: Defines an invariant: good loiter performance requires all three conditions to hold.

property.property_specification: If a good GPS lock, low magnetic interference on the compass and low vibrations are present, then good loiter performance will eventually be achieved.
property.reason: Expresses the relationship that if the conditions (good GPS lock, low magnetic interference, and low vibrations) hold, this leads to good loiter performance eventually.

property.property_specification: The pilot can possibly control the copter's position using the control sticks.
property.reason: The text indicates a possibility ("can"), which translates to a reachability property (E<>) where the copter reaches a controlled state.

property.property_specification: It is possible for the copter to be in a state of horizontal or vertical movement controlled by the pilot.
property.reason: The pilot's ability to control horizontal/vertical movement implies a possible state where at least one control axis is active (E<> represents possible reachability).

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is armed in Loiter mode, GPS must have 3D lock and HDOP must be below 1.4.
property.reason: This represents an invariant (A[]) ensuring the arming condition in Loiter mode always requires GPS 3D lock and HDOP < 1.4.

property.property_specification: Whenever the vehicle is in Loiter mode and GPS has 3D lock and HDOP is below 1.4, the vehicle will eventually be armed.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: When the vehicle satisfies the Loiter, GPS lock, and HDOP condition it leads to the 'Armed' state.

property.property_specification: It is always true that if the system is armed in PosHold mode, then the GPS has 3D lock and HDOP is 2.0 or lower.
property.reason: The arming action in PosHold mode is strictly conditional on GPS_3D_lock and HDOP <= 2.0. This is a safety invariant ensuring the system never arms in PosHold mode without satisfying these prerequisites.

property.property_specification: Whenever the system is in PosHold mode, GPS has 3D lock, and HDOP is 2.0 or lower, arming will eventually occur.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: If certain conditions (PosHold mode, GPS 3D lock, HDOP <= 2.0) are met, arming will eventually happen.

property.property_specification: The LED will eventually become green in all executions.
property.reason: The statement 'will become green' implies that the LED must transition to green inevitably (i.e., in all possible paths), which corresponds to the UPPAAL 'A<>' (eventually) operator.

property.property_specification: Eventually the LED will become green.
property.reason: The statement indicates that the LED will inevitably turn green, which translates to 'eventually' becoming green.

property.property_specification: When RTL mode is selected and rally points have been setup, the copter will eventually return to the closest rally point.
property.reason: Expresses that triggering RTL mode with rally points setup leads to the copter reaching the closest rally point.

property.property_specification: When RTL mode is selected and rally points have not been setup, the copter will eventually return to the home location.
property.reason: Expresses that triggering RTL mode without rally points setup leads to the copter reaching the home location.

property.property_specification: When RTL mode is selected, the copter will eventually return to the home location or the closest rally point.
property.reason: The statement describes a 'leads to' relationship: if RTL mode is selected, then eventually the copter will be at the home location or the closest rally point.

property.property_specification: Whenever WPNAV_RFND_USE is 1 and Terrain is selected, the rangefinder will eventually be used.
property.reason: Uses the leads-to operator (-->) to enforce that the conjunction of WPNAV_RFND_USE=1 and Terrain selection eventually triggers rangefinder usage.

property.property_specification: If WPNAV_RFND_USE = 1 and Terrain is selected, then rangefinder will be used.

property.property_specification: The motor interlock must be disabled to reset the governor.

property.property_specification: The governor disengages when the throttle is at 20%.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: when throttle level is 20, it leads to governor disengagement.

property.property_specification: If the RPM falls outside the specified range, the governor will eventually disengage.

property.property_specification: If the system over-runs on a sudden punchout, it leads to reducing the TC gain.

property.property_specification: Dis-engaging the motor interlock sets the RSC output to H_RSC_IDLE.

property.property_specification: Spool down completion leads to auto-disarm and engine shutdown.

property.property_specification: Whenever the helicopter is being handled, the drive must be disconnected.

property.property_specification: Whenever the helicopter is rolled to the left, the swash plate eventually leans to the right.

property.property_specification: Excessive vibration can potentially lead to attitude control errors or EKF Failsafe.

property.property_specification: If the aircraft is a dual rotor helicopter, the ATC_HOVR_ROL_TRM should eventually be set to zero.

property.property_specification: If the first low in-place hover was successful, then the dynamic harmonic notch filter is eventually set up.

In UPPAAL, this 'leads to' relationship is directly captured by the --> operator, which enforces that whenever the bandwidth condition holds, the effectiveness eventually holds in all paths. While the text uses 'may' (suggesting possibility), UPPAAL's --> operator encodes inevitability once the condition is met, aligning with the technical context where sufficient bandwidth ensures effectiveness.

property.property_specification: If the bandwidth of the notches, INS_HNTCH_BW, are wide enough, the harmonic notch setup for the 4th harmonic may still be effective.

property.property_specification: If the actual rate is more than the desired rate, then ATC_RAT_xxx_FF will eventually be decreased.

property.property_specification: If the aircraft is tuning the ATC_RAT_xxx_FF gain and starts oscillating, then eventually the oscillations will stop.

property.property_specification: After the final gain is set, the system remains stable during hover testing.

property.property_specification: After tuning the ATC_RAT_xxx_D and ATC_RAT_xxx_P gains, the aircraft eventually becomes smoother.

property.property_specification: After tuning the ATC_RAT_xxx_D and ATC_RAT_xxx_P gains, the aircraft should feel much smoother.

property.property_specification: If the tail seems 'loose' (tail_loose state), then eventually ATC_RAT_YAW_P is increased (ATC_RAT_YAW_P_high).

property.property_specification: If the tail slowly wags (tail_slow_wag state), then ATC_RAT_YAW_P may be too high (ATC_RAT_YAW_P_high).

property.property_specification: If the tail seems “loose” and doesn’t want to hold, then eventually the ATC_RAT_YAW_P will increase.

property.property_specification: If the aircraft drifts more than 10 meters during dwell or frequency sweep tests, AUTOTUNE_VELXY_P should eventually be increased.
