property.property_specification: Whenever the ArduCopter control laws are in Acro mode, the software converts the pilot or autopilot’s input into a pilot demanded axial rate.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the system is in 'Acro', it will eventually lead to 'pilot_demanded_axial_rate'.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the heli maintains a near driftless hover (< 1 m/s).
property.reason: Expresses the eventual goal of the heli maintaining a near driftless hover as an invariant property. Assuming 'hover' represents the state where the heli maintains a near driftless hover.

property.property_specification: On a calm day, the heli will maintain near driftless hover eventually.
property.reason: Expresses that on a calm day, the heli will inevitably maintain a near driftless hover.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the helicopter is stable.
property.reason: Expresses that the helicopter will eventually reach a stable state.

property.property_specification: Eventually the helicopter is in stabilize flight mode.
property.reason: The helicopter inevitably enters stabilize flight mode.

property.property_specification: It is always the case that ATC_ANG_YAW_P should not be below 3.5.
property.reason: Expresses the invariant that the value of ATC_ANG_YAW_P should always be greater than or equal to 3.5.

property.property_specification: It is always the case that ATC_RAT_YAW_P should not be above 0.38.
property.reason: Expresses the invariant that the value of ATC_RAT_YAW_P should always be less than or equal to 0.38.

property.property_specification: If the system is a flybar head system, the rate D gain is zero.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship that if it's a flybar head, then the rate D gain will be zero.

property.property_specification: If the system is a flybar head system, the rate P gain is zero.
property.reason: Expresses the 'leads to' relationship that if it's a flybar head, then the rate P gain will be zero.

property.property_specification: The amplitude of the peaks should be approximately the same.
property.reason: This states an invariant property about the amplitude of peaks in the system that should always hold.

property.property_specification: The amplitude of the peaks should be approximately the same.
property.reason: This states that there exists a path where the amplitude of peaks is approximately the same.

property.property_specification: There is no more than 100 milliseconds offset between the peaks.
property.reason: Expresses a time constraint that the difference between peak times should be no more than 100 time units (assuming time is measured in milliseconds and 'peak2_time' and 'peak1_time' hold time values).

property.property_specification: There is no more than 100 milliseconds offset between the peaks.
property.reason: Expresses a time constraint that the difference between peak times should be no more than 100 time units (assuming time is measured in milliseconds and 'peak2_time' and 'peak1_time' hold time values).

property.property_specification: PWM for maximum collective pitch on aft rotor head corresponds to H_COL_ANG_MAX.
property.reason: This describes a state where the PWM for the maximum collective pitch (H_COL2_MAX_PWM) is equal to H_COL_ANG_MAX, which can be represented by an equality.

property.property_specification: At maximum collective pitch on the left rotor head, the PWM is H_COL_MAX which corresponds to H_COL_ANG_MAX.
property.reason: Expresses that if the PWM for maximum collective pitch is achieved, it will lead to reaching H_COL_ANG_MAX.

property.property_specification: When the PWM for the maximum collective pitch on the counter-clockwise rotor head is at its maximum (H_COL_MAX), the collective pitch angle is at its maximum (H_COL_ANG_MAX). Representing this relationship directly as a temporal property might not be meaningful without further context.
property.reason: This statement describes a static relationship between two variables at their maximum values. While it implies a connection, it does not inherently represent a temporal property that can be directly expressed as an UPPAAL formula without additional information about timing or state transitions. It is a statement of correlation rather than a statement of sequence or reachability.

property.property_specification: It is possible that the copter yaws right on take-off.
property.reason: Expresses the possibility of the copter yawing right.

property.property_specification: It is possible that the copter yaws left on take-off.
property.reason: Expresses the possibility of the copter yawing left.

property.property_specification: Eventually, the AT will rapidly spin itself around 360 degrees to keep the antennas on target if the panning range is at the limit of travel.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship where reaching the panning limit leads to the antennas being on target.

property.property_specification: Always, if the panning range is at the limit of travel, the AT will rapidly spin itself around 360 degrees to keep the antennas on target.
property.reason: Cannot be represented in UPPAAL because the nesting of temporal operators is not allowed.

property.property_specification: After setting up AT outside pointing north and firing it up, and walking around the AT with the plane linked up as if in flight, it is necessary to get at least 60 feet away.
property.reason: This describes a setup procedure and a requirement but does not specify a temporal property that can be expressed as an UPPAAL formula.

property.property_specification: After setting up AT outside pointing north and firing it up, and walking around the AT with the plane linked up as if in flight, try to get 100ft away to ensure.
property.reason: This describes a setup procedure and a suggestion to try getting 100ft away, but does not specify a temporal property that can be expressed as an UPPAAL formula.

property.property_specification: Running the sliders up and down ensures those parameters are set the way they should be.
property.reason: Expresses that moving the sliders leads to the parameters being correctly set.

property.property_specification: Running the sliders up and down eventually ensures those parameters are set the way they should be.
property.reason: Expresses that after the slider is run, the parameters will eventually be set.

property.property_specification: It is possible for the Scout drone's flight parameters to be modified.
property.reason: Expresses the possibility of the Scout drone's parameters being modified.

property.property_specification: It is possible for the Journey drone's flight parameters to be modified.
property.reason: Expresses the possibility of the Journey drone's parameters being modified.

property.property_specification: The camera shutter needs to be triggered always.
property.reason: Expressed the requirement that the shutter is always triggered by interpreting 'throughout the mission' as 'always'.

property.property_specification: The camera shutter needs to be triggered potentially always.
property.reason: Expressed the requirement that the shutter is always triggered by interpreting 'throughout the mission' as 'potentially always'.

property.property_specification: Whenever an input signal is detected and verified, PPM output will eventually be enabled.
property.reason: Expresses a leads-to relationship: if the input signal is detected and verified, then PPM output will eventually be enabled.

property.property_specification: If an input signal has been detected, then PPM output will eventually be enabled.
property.reason: This states that detection of the input signal leads to the PPM output being enabled eventually.

property.property_specification: The throttle is set to low (900μs).
property.reason: This statement describes a system state; the throttle is in a 'low' state.

property.property_specification: throttle is set to low.
property.reason: Represents a state where the throttle is low.

property.property_specification: From now on, the file "OSD_Charset.mcm" will always be there.
property.reason: The text indicates an invariant: the file "OSD_Charset.mcm" will always be available.

property.property_specification: From now on, OSD_Charset.mcm will always be there.
property.reason: Expresses that the state 'OSD_Charset_mcm_exists' is always true, meaning the file is always present.

property.property_specification: The UAV must always operate away from obstructions.
property.reason: Expresses that the UAV must always be away from obstructions. Assuming 'nearObstruction' is a boolean variable that is true when the UAV is near an obstruction, it's negation ensures the UAV is *not* near an obstruction.

property.property_specification: The UAV must always operate away from populated areas.
property.reason: Expresses that the UAV must always be away from populated areas. Assuming 'inPopulatedArea' is a boolean variable that is true when the UAV is in a populated area, it's negation ensures the UAV is *not* in a populated area.

property.property_specification: In a closed-loop simulation, the hardware autopilot feeding data back to the software simulator will eventually occur.
property.reason: Expresses that in a closed-loop configuration, the autopilot will eventually send data back to the simulator.

property.property_specification: If the simulation is a closed-loop simulation, the hardware autopilot feeding data back to the software simulator will eventually occur.
property.reason: Expresses that if the simulation is set up as closed loop, then eventually data will be sent back from the autopilot to the simulator.
